[!class-level|monk] Level 17 [!class-feature|monk] Elemental Epitome Level 17 Warrior of the Elements Feature While your Elemental Attunement is active, you gain the following benefits: Damage Resistance. You gain Resistance to Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage of your choice. At the start of each of your turns, you can change the chosen damage type. Destructive Stride. When you use Step of the Wind, your Speed increases by 20 feet until the end of the turn. During that movement, any creature of your choice takes damage equal to one roll of your Martial Arts die when you enter a space within 5 feet of it. The damage type is your choice of Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. A creature can take this damage only once per turn. Empowered Strikes. Once on each of your turns, when you hit a creature with an Unarmed Strike, you can deal extra damage equal to one roll of your Martial Arts die. The extra damage is the same type dealt by the strike.

title: Monk
content: [!class-header|monk] Monk [!class-divider|monk] [!button-pack|monk] Monk Summary Monk Features Monk Subclasses [!class-divider|monk] [!class-recite|monk] A Martial Artist of Supernatural Focus Monks use rigorous combat training and mental discipline to align themselves with the multiverse and focus their internal reservoirs of power. Different Monks conceptualize this power in various ways: as breath, energy, life force, essence, or self. Whether channeled as a striking display of martial prowess or as a subtler manifestation of defense and speed, this power infuses all that a Monk does. Monks focus their internal power to create extraordinary, even supernatural, effects. They channel uncanny speed and strength into their attacks, with or without the use of weapons. In a Monk’s hands, even the most basic weapons can become sophisticated implements of combat mastery. Many Monks find that a structured life of ascetic withdrawal helps them cultivate the physical and mental focus they need to harness their power. Others believe that immersing themselves in the vibrant confusion of life fuels their determination and discipline. Monks generally view adventures as tests of their physical and mental development. They strive to become living weapons rather than merely slayers of monsters and collectors of treasure. [!infobox-class|monk wfull] Core Monk Traits --- --- Primary Ability Dexterity and Wisdom Hit Dice Die D8 per Monk level Saving Throws Proficiencies Strength and Dexterity Skill Proficiencies Choose 2: Acrobatics, Athletics, History, Insight, Religion, or Stealth Weapon Proficiencies Simple weapons and Martial weapons that have the Light property Tool Proficiencies Choose one type of Artisan’s Tools or Musical Instrument Armor Training None Starting Equipment Choose A or B: (A) Spear, 5 Daggers, Artisan’s Tools or Musical Instrument chosen for the tool proficiency above, Explorer’s Pack, and 11 GP; or (B) 50 GP [!class-divider|monk small] [!class-header|monk] Becoming a Monk [!class-header|monk] As a Level 1 Character Gain all the traits in the Core Monk Traits table. Gain the Monk’s level 1 features, which are listed in the Monk Features table. [!class-header|monk] As a Multiclass Character Gain the Hit Point Die trait from the Core Monk Traits table. Gain the Monk’s level 1 features, which are listed in the Monk Features table. [!class-divider|monk small] [!class-header|monk] Monk Class Features As a Monk, you gain the following class features when you reach the specified Monk levels. These features are listed in the Monk Features table. [!class-table|monk] Level Prof. Bonus Class Features Martial Arts Focus Points Unarmored Movement 1 +2 Martial Arts, Unarmored Defense 1d6 — — 2 +2 Monk’s Focus, Unarmored Movement, Uncanny Metabolism 1d6 2 +10 ft. 3 +2 Deflect Attacks, Monk Subclass 1d6 3 +10 ft. 4 +2 Ability Score Improvement, Slow Fall 1d6 4 +10 ft. 5 +3 Extra Attack, Stunning Strike 1d8 5 +10 ft. 6 +3 Empowered Strikes, Subclass Feature 1d8 6 +15 ft. 7 +3 Evasion 1d8 7 +15 ft. 8 +3 Ability Score Improvement 1d8 8 +15 ft. 9 +4 Acrobatic Movement 1d8 9 +15 ft. 10 +4 Heightened Focus, Self-Restoration 1d8 10 +20 ft. 11 +4 Subclass Feature 1d10 11 +20 ft. 12 +4 Ability Score Improvement 1d10 12 +20 ft. 13 +5 Deflect Energy 1d10 13 +20 ft. 14 +5 Disciplined Survivor 1d10 14 +25 ft. 15 +5 Perfect Focus 1d10 15 +25 ft. 16 +5 Ability Score Improvement 1d10 16 +25 ft. 17 +6 Subclass Feature 1d12 17 +25 ft. 18 +6 Superior Defense 1d12 18 +30 ft. 19 +6 Epic Boon 1d12 19 +30 ft. 20 +6 Body and Mind 1d12 20 +30 ft.

title: Paladin Features
content: [!class-header|paladin] Paladin Feature List [!class-divider|paladin] [!button-pack|paladin] Paladin Paladin Features Paladin Subclasses Paladin Spell List [!class-divider|paladin] [!class-level|paladin] Level 1 [!class-feature|paladin] Lay On Hands Level 1 Paladin Feature Your blessed touch can heal wounds. You have a pool of healing power that replenishes when you finish a Long Rest. With that pool, you can restore a total number of Hit Points equal to five times your Paladin level. As a Bonus Action, you can touch a creature (which could be yourself) and draw power from the pool to restore a number of Hit Points to that creature, up to the maximum amount remaining in the pool. You can also expend 5 Hit Points from the pool to remove the Poisoned condition from the creature. Those points don’t also restore Hit Points. [!class-divider|paladin small] [!class-feature|paladin] Spellcasting Level 1 Paladin Feature You have learned to cast spells through prayer and meditation. Spell Slots. The Paladin Features table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended slots when you finish a Long Rest. Prepared Spells of Level 1+. You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose two level 1 Paladin spells. Heroism and Searing Smite are recommended. The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Paladin levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Paladin Features table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional Paladin spells until the number of spells on your list matches the number in the table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. If another Paladin feature gives you spells that you always have prepared, those spells don’t count against the number of spells you can prepare with this feature. Changing Your Prepared Spells. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can replace one spell on your list with another Paladin spell for which you have spell slots. Spellcasting Ability. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for your Paladin spells. Spellcasting Focus. You can use a Holy Symbol as a Spellcasting Focus for your Paladin spells. [!class-divider|paladin small] [!class-feature|paladin] Weapon Mastery Level 1 Paladin Feature Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change those weapon choices. [!class-level|paladin] Level 2 [!class-feature|paladin] Fighting Style Level 2 Paladin Feature You gain a Fighting Style feat of your choice. Instead of choosing one of those feats, you can choose the option below. Blessed Warrior. You learn two Cleric cantrips of your choice. Guidance and Sacred Flame are recommended. The chosen cantrips count as Paladin spells for you, and Charisma is your spellcasting ability for them. Whenever you gain a Paladin level, you can replace one of these cantrips with another Cleric cantrip. [!class-divider|paladin small] [!class-feature|paladin] Paladin's Smite Level 2 Paladin Feature You always have the Divine Smite spell prepared. In addition, you can cast it without expending a spell slot, but you must finish a Long Rest before you can cast it in this way again. [!class-level|paladin] Level 3 [!class-feature|paladin] Channel Divinity Level 3 Paladin Feature You can channel divine energy directly from the Outer Planes, using it to fuel magical effects. You start with one such effect: Divine Sense. You can use this class’s Channel Divinity twice. You regain one expended use when you finish a Short Rest, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. You gain an additional use when you reach Paladin level 11. If a Channel Divinity effect requires a saving throw, the DC equals the spell save DC from this class’s Spellcasting feature. Divine Sense. As a Bonus Action, you can open your awareness to detect Celestials, Fiends, and Undead. For the next 10 minutes or until you have the Incapacitated condition, you know the location of any creature of those types within 60 feet of yourself and know its creature type. Within the same radius, you also detect the presence of any place or object that has been consecrated or desecrated, as with the Hallow spell. [!class-divider|paladin small] [!class-feature|paladin] Paladin Subclass Level 3 Paladin Feature You gain a Paladin subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Paladin levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass’s features that are of your Paladin level or lower. The following subclasses are available: Oath of Devotion Oath of Glory Oath of the Ancients Oath of Vengeance [!info] Breaking Your Oath A Paladin tries to hold to the highest standards of conduct, but even the most dedicated are fallible. Sometimes a Paladin transgresses their oath. A Paladin who has broken a vow typically seeks absolution, spending an all-night vigil as a sign of penitence or undertaking a fast. After a rite of forgiveness, the Paladin starts fresh. If your Paladin unrepentantly violates their oath, talk to your DM. Your Paladin should probably take a more appropriate subclass or even abandon the class and adopt another one. [!class-level|paladin] Level 4 [!class-feature|paladin] Ability Score Improvement Level 4 Paladin Feature You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Paladin levels 8, 12, and 16. [!class-level|paladin] Level 5 [!class-feature|paladin] Extra Attack Level 5 Paladin Feature You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn. [!class-divider|paladin small] [!class-feature|paladin] Faithful Steed Level 5 Paladin Feature You always have the Find Steed spell prepared. You can also cast the spell once without expending a spell slot. You regain the ability to do so when you finish a Long Rest. [!class-level|paladin] Level 6 [!class-feature|paladin] Aura of Protection Level 6 Paladin Feature You radiate a protective aura in a 10-foot Emanation that originates from you. The aura is inactive while you have the Incapacitated condition. You and your allies in the aura gain a bonus to saving throws equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum +1). If another Paladin is present, a creature can benefit from only one Aura of Protection at a time. [!class-level|paladin] Level 9 [!class-feature|paladin] Abjure Foes Level 9 Paladin Feature As a Magic action, you can expend one use of Channel Divinity to overwhelm foes with awe. Each target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the Frightened condition for 1 minute or until it takes damage. [!class-level|paladin] Level 10 [!class-feature|paladin] Aura of Courage Level 10 Paladin Feature You and your allies have Immunity to the Frightened condition while in your Aura of Protection. If a Frightened ally enters the aura, that condition has no effect on that ally while there. [!class-level|paladin] Level 11 [!class-feature|paladin] Radiant Strikes Level 11 Paladin Feature When you hit a target with an attack roll using a Melee weapon or an Unarmed Strike, the target takes an extra 1d8 Radiant damage. [!class-level|paladin] Level 14 [!class-feature|paladin] Restoring Touch Level 14 Paladin Feature When you use Lay On Hands on a creature, you can also remove one or more of the following conditions: Blinded Charmed Deafened Frightened Paralyzed Stunned You must expend 5 Hit Points from the Lay On Hands pool for each condition removed. [!class-level|paladin] Level 18 [!class-feature|paladin] Aura Expansion Level 18 Paladin Feature Your Aura of Protection is now a 30-foot Emanation. [!class-level|paladin] Level 19 [!class-feature|paladin] Epic Boon Level 19 Paladin Feature You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. Boon of Truesight is recommended.

title: Paladin Spell List
content: [!class-header|paladin] Paladin Spell List [!class-divider|paladin] [!button-pack|paladin] Paladin Summary Paladin Features Paladin Subclasses Paladin Spell List [!class-divider|paladin] This section presents the Paladin spell list. The spells are organized by spell level and then alphabetized, and each spell’s school of magic is listed. In the Special column: C means the spell requires Concentration. R means the spell is a Ritual. M means the spell requires a specific Material component. [!class-header|paladin] Level 1 Paladin Spells [!class-table|paladin] Spell School Special Bless Enchantment C, M Command Enchantment — Compelled Duel Enchantment C Cure Wounds Abjuration — Detect Evil and Good Divination C Detect Magic Divination C, R Detect Poison and Disease Divination C, R Divine Favor Transmutation — Divine Smite Evocation — Heroism Enchantment C Protection from Evil and Good Abjuration C, M Purify Food and Drink Transmutation R Searing Smite Evocation — Shield of Faith Abjuration C Thunderous Smite Evocation — Wrathful Smite Necromancy — [!class-header|paladin] Level 2 Paladin Spells [!class-table|paladin] Spell School Special Aid Abjuration — Find Steed Conjuration — Gentle Repose Necromancy R, M Lesser Restoration Abjuration — Locate Object Divination C Magic Weapon Transmutation — Prayer of Healing Abjuration — Protection from Poison Abjuration — Shining Smite Transmutation C Warding Bond Abjuration M Zone of Truth Enchantment — [!class-header|paladin] Level 3 Paladin Spells [!class-table|paladin] Spell School Special Aura of Vitality Abjuration C Blinding Smite Evocation — Create Food and Water Conjuration — Crusader’s Mantle Transmutation C Daylight Evocation — Dispel Magic Abjuration — Elemental Weapon Transmutation C Magic Circle Abjuration M Remove Curse Abjuration — Revivify Necromancy M [!class-header|paladin] Level 4 Paladin Spells [!class-table|paladin] Spell School Special Aura of Life Abjuration C Aura of Purity Abjuration C Banishment Abjuration C Death Ward Abjuration — Locate Creature Divination C Staggering Smite Enchantment — [!class-header|paladin] Level 5 Paladin Spells [!class-table|paladin] Spell School Special Banishing Smite Conjuration C Circle of Power Abjuration C Destructive Wave Evocation — Dispel Evil and Good Abjuration C Geas Enchantment — Greater Restoration Abjuration M Raise Dead Necromancy M Summon Celestial Conjuration C, M

title: Oath of Devotion
content: [!class-header|paladin] Oath of Devotion [!class-divider|paladin] [!button-pack|paladin] Paladin Paladin Features Paladin Subclasses Paladin Spell List [!class-divider|paladin] !OathofDevotion_Banner.png [!class-recite|paladin] Uphold the Ideals of Justice and Order The Oath of Devotion binds Paladins to the ideals of justice and order. These Paladins embody the archetype of the knight in shining armor. They hold themselves to the highest standards of conduct, and some, for better or worse, hold the rest of the world to those same standards. Many who swear this oath are devoted to gods of law and good and use their gods’ tenets as the measure of personal devotion. Others hold angels as their ideals and incorporate images of angelic wings into their helmets or coats of arms. Devotion Paladins commonly follow these tenets: Let your word be your promise. Protect the weak and never fear to act. Let your honorable deeds be an example. [!class-level|paladin] Level 3 [!class-feature|paladin] Oath of Devotion Spells Level 3 Oath of Devotion Feature The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells prepared. When you reach the specified Paladin levels, you thereafter always have the following spells prepared. [!class-table|paladin] Paladin Level Prepared Spells 3 Protection from Evil and Good, Shield of Faith 5 Aid, Zone of Truth 9 Beacon of Hope, Dispel Magic 13 Freedom of Movement, Guardian of Faith 17 Commune, Flame Strike [!class-divider|paladin small] [!class-feature|paladin] Sacred Weapon Level 3 Oath of Devotion Feature When you take the Attack action, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to imbue one Melee weapon you are holding with positive energy. For 10 minutes, or until you use this feature again, you gain the following benefits: Add your Charisma modifier to attack rolls made with that weapon (minimum bonus of +1). Whenever you hit with the weapon, you can cause it to deal either its normal damage type or Radiant damage. The weapon also emits Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 20 feet. You can end this effect early (no action required). The effect also ends if you stop carrying the weapon. [!class-level|paladin] Level 7 [!class-feature|paladin] Aura of Devotion Level 7 Oath of Devotion Feature You and your allies have Immunity to the Charmed condition while within your Aura of Protection. If a Charmed ally enters the aura, the condition has no effect on that ally while they remain within it. [!class-level|paladin] Level 15 [!class-feature|paladin] Smite of Protection Level 15 Oath of Devotion Feature Your divine smites radiate protective energy. Whenever you cast Divine Smite, you and your allies gain Half Cover while within your Aura of Protection. This benefit lasts until the start of your next turn. [!class-level|paladin] Level 20 [!class-feature|paladin] Holy Nimbus Level 20 Oath of Devotion Feature As a Bonus Action, you can imbue your Aura of Protection with holy power for 10 minutes or until you end the effect (no action required). Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest. You can also restore its use by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required). While the effect is active, you gain the following benefits: Holy Ward. You have Advantage on saving throws forced by Fiends or Undead. Radiant Damage. Whenever an enemy starts its turn within your Aura of Protection, it takes Radiant damage equal to your Charisma modifier plus your Proficiency Bonus. Sunlight. Your aura is filled with Bright Light that counts as sunlight.

title: Oath of Glory
content: [!class-header|paladin] Oath of Glory [!class-divider|paladin] [!button-pack|paladin] Paladin Paladin Features Paladin Subclasses Paladin Spell List [!class-divider|paladin] !OathofGlory_Banner.png [!class-recite|paladin] Strive for the Heights of Heroism Paladins who take the Oath of Glory believe they and their companions are destined to achieve glory through deeds of heroism. They train diligently and encourage their companions so they are ready when destiny calls. Glory Paladins commonly follow these tenets: Endeavor to be known by your deeds. Face hardships with courage. Inspire others to strive for glory. [!class-level|paladin] Level 3 [!class-feature|paladin] Inspiring Smite Level 3 Oath of Glory Feature Immediately after you cast Divine Smite, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity and distribute Temporary Hit Points to creatures of your choice within 30 feet of yourself, including yourself. The total number of Temporary Hit Points equals 2d8 plus your Paladin level. Divide those Temporary Hit Points among the chosen creatures however you like. [!class-divider|paladin small] [!class-feature|paladin] Oath of Glory Spells Level 3 Oath of Glory Feature The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells prepared. When you reach the specified Paladin levels, you thereafter always have the following spells prepared. [!class-table|paladin] Paladin Level Prepared Spells 3 Guiding Bolt, Heroism 5 Enhance Ability, Magic Weapon 9 Haste, Protection from Energy 13 Compulsion, Freedom of Movement 17 Legend Lore, Yolande’s Regal Presence [!class-divider|paladin small] [!class-feature|paladin] Peerless Athlete Level 3 Oath of Glory Feature As a Bonus Action, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to augment your athleticism. For 1 hour, you gain the following benefits: Advantage on Strength (Athletics) checks. Advantage on Dexterity (Acrobatics) checks. Your Long Jump and High Jump distances increase by 10 feet. This additional jump distance costs movement as normal. [!class-level|paladin] Level 7 [!class-feature|paladin] Aura of Alacrity Level 7 Oath of Glory Feature Your Speed increases by 10 feet. In addition, whenever an ally enters your Aura of Protection for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, that ally’s Speed increases by 10 feet until the end of its next turn. [!class-level|paladin] Level 15 [!class-feature|paladin] Glorious Defense Level 15 Oath of Glory Feature You can turn defense into a sudden strike. When you or another creature you can see within 10 feet of yourself is hit by an attack roll, you can take a Reaction to grant a bonus to the target’s AC against that attack. The bonus equals your Charisma modifier (minimum of +1), potentially causing the attack to miss. If the attack misses, you can immediately make one attack with a weapon against the attacker as part of the same Reaction, provided the attacker is within your weapon’s range. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. [!class-level|paladin] Level 20 [!class-feature|paladin] Living Legend Level 20 Oath of Glory Feature You can empower yourself with the legends, whether true or exaggerated, of your heroic deeds. As a Bonus Action, you gain the following benefits for 10 minutes: Charismatic. You have Advantage on all Charisma checks. Saving Throw Reroll. If you fail a saving throw, you can take a Reaction to reroll it. You must use the new roll. Unerring Strike. Once on each of your turns, when you miss with an attack roll made using a weapon, you can cause that attack to hit instead. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest. You can also restore its use by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required).

title: Oath of Vengeance
content: [!class-header|paladin] Oath of Vengeance [!class-divider|paladin] [!button-pack|paladin] Paladin Paladin Features Paladin Subclasses Paladin Spell List [!class-divider|paladin] !OathofVengeance_Banner.png [!class-recite|paladin] Punish Evildoers at Any Cost The Oath of Vengeance is a solemn commitment to punish those who have committed grievously evil acts. When evil armies slaughter helpless villagers, when a tyrant defies the will of the gods, when a thieves’ guild grows too violent, or when a dragon rampages through the countryside, Paladins arise and swear an Oath of Vengeance to set right what has gone wrong. These Paladins commonly follow these tenets: Show the wicked no mercy. Fight injustice and its causes. Aid those harmed by injustice. [!class-level|paladin] Level 3 [!class-feature|paladin] Oath of Vengeance Spells Level 3 Oath of Vengeance Feature The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells prepared. When you reach the specified Paladin levels, you thereafter always have the following spells prepared. [!class-table|paladin] Paladin Level Prepared Spells 3 Bane, Hunter’s Mark 1 5 Hold Person, Misty Step 9 Haste, Protection from Energy 13 Banishment, Dimension Door 17 Hold Monster, Scrying [!class-divider|paladin small] [!class-feature|paladin] Vow of Enmity Level 3 Oath of Vengeance Feature When you take the Attack action, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to utter a vow of enmity against a creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. You have Advantage on attack rolls against that creature for 1 minute or until you use this feature again. If the creature drops to 0 Hit Points before the vow ends, you can transfer the vow to a different creature within 30 feet of yourself (no action required). [!class-level|paladin] Level 7 [!class-feature|paladin] Relentless Avenger Level 7 Oath of Vengeance Feature Your supernatural focus helps you close off a foe’s retreat. When you hit a creature with an Opportunity Attack, you can reduce that creature’s Speed to 0 until the end of the current turn. You can then move up to half your Speed as part of the same Reaction. This movement doesn’t provoke Opportunity Attacks. [!class-level|paladin] Level 15 [!class-feature|paladin] Soul of Vengeance Level 15 Oath of Vengeance Feature Immediately after a creature under the effect of your Vow of Enmity hits or misses with an attack roll, you can take a Reaction to make a melee attack against that creature if it is within range. [!class-level|paladin] Level 20 [!class-feature|paladin] Avenging Angel Level 20 Oath of Vengeance Feature As a Bonus Action, you gain the following benefits for 10 minutes or until you end them (no action required). Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest. You can also restore its use by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required). While this feature is active, you gain the following benefits: Flight. You sprout spectral wings, gain a Fly Speed of 60 feet, and can hover. Frightful Aura. Whenever an enemy starts its turn within your Aura of Protection, it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or gain the Frightened condition for 1 minute or until it takes any damage. Attack rolls against a creature Frightened by this feature have Advantage.

title: Oath of the Noble Genies
content: [!class-header|paladin] Oath of the Noble Genies [!class-divider|paladin] [!button-pack|paladin] Paladin Paladin Features Paladin Subclasses [!class-divider|paladin] !Oath_of_the_Noble_Genies_Banner.png [!class-recite|paladin] Brandish the Elemental Splendor of Genies Paladins sworn to the Oath of the Noble Genies revere the forces of the Elemental Planes. Through taking this oath, Paladins draw power from the four great genie kinds: Dao, masters of earth Djinn, masters of air Efreet, masters of fire Marids, masters of water Many who swear this oath hail from Calimshan, a land rich with genie influence. These paladins share the following tenets: Sow the seeds of creation amid the ashes of destruction. Lead with splendor and grace. Respect the elements, and fear their wrath. [!class-level|paladin] Level 3 [!class-feature|paladin] Elemental Smite Level 3 Oath of the Noble Genies Feature Immediately after you cast Divine Smite, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity and invoke one of the following effects: Dao's Crush. Earth rises around the target of your Divine Smite. The target gains the Grappled condition (escape DC equals your spell save DC). While Grappled in this way, the target also has the Restrained condition. Djinni's Escape. You teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet. Until the end of your next turn, you gain: Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage Immunity to the Grappled condition Immunity to the Prone condition Immunity to the Restrained condition Efreeti's Fury. The target of your Divine Smite takes an extra 2d4 Fire damage. Fire then leaps to another creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. That creature also takes 2d4 Fire damage. Marid's Surge. The target of your Divine Smite and each creature of your choice in a 10-foot Emanation originating from you must make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, a creature: Is pushed 15 feet directly away from you. Gains the Prone condition. [!class-divider|paladin small] [!class-feature|paladin] Genie Spells Level 3 Oath of the Noble Genies Feature The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Paladin level specified in the Genie Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared. Paladin Level Spells 3 Chromatic Orb, Elementalism, Thunderous Smite 5 Mirror Image, Phantasmal Force 9 Fly, Gaseous Form 13 Conjure Minor Elementals, Summon Elemental 17 Banishing Smite, Contact Other Plane [!class-divider|paladin small] [!class-feature|paladin] Genie's Splendor Level 3 Oath of the Noble Genies Feature While you aren't wearing armor, your base Armor Class equals: 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Charisma modifier You can use a Shield and still gain this benefit. In addition, you gain proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: Acrobatics Intimidation Performance Persuasion [!class-level|paladin] Level 7 [!class-feature|paladin] Aura of Elemental Shielding Level 7 Oath of the Noble Genies Feature Choose one of the following damage types: Acid Cold Fire Lightning Thunder You and your allies have Resistance to the chosen damage type while within your Aura of Protection. At the start of each of your turns, you can change the chosen damage type to another option from the list (no action required). [!class-level|paladin] Level 15 [!class-feature|paladin] Elemental Rebuke Level 15 Oath of the Noble Genies Feature When you are hit by an attack roll, you can take a Reaction to halve the attack's damage against you (round down). The attacker must then make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the attacker takes: 2d10 + your Charisma modifier damage of a type of your choice: Acid Cold Fire Lightning Thunder On a successful save, the attacker takes half as much damage. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. [!class-level|paladin] Level 20 [!class-feature|paladin] Noble Scion Level 20 Oath of the Noble Genies Feature As a Bonus Action, you gain the following benefits for 10 minutes or until you end them (no action required). Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest. You can also restore your use by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required). Flight. You gain a Fly Speed of 60 feet and can hover. Minor Wish. When you or an ally within your Aura of Protection fails a D20 Test, you can take a Reaction to cause that creature to succeed instead.

content: [!class-header|paladin] Paladin Subclasses [!class-divider|paladin] [!button-pack|paladin] Paladin Summary Paladin Features Paladin Subclasses Paladin Spell List [!cards|3] Oath of Devotion Oath of Glory Oath of the Ancients Oath of Vengeance Oath of the Noble Genies

title: Paladin
content: [!class-header|paladin] Paladin [!class-divider|paladin] [!button-pack|paladin] Paladin Summary Paladin Features Paladin Subclasses Paladin Spell List [!class-divider|paladin] [!class-recite|paladin] A Devout Warrior of Sacred Oaths Paladins are united by their oaths to stand against the forces of annihilation and corruption. Whether sworn before a god’s altar, in a sacred glade before nature spirits, or in a moment of desperation and grief with the dead as the only witnesses, a Paladin’s oath is a powerful bond. It is a source of power that turns a devout warrior into a blessed champion. Paladins train to learn the skills of combat, mastering a variety of weapons and armor. Even so, their martial skills are secondary to the magical power they wield: power to heal the injured, smite their foes, and protect the helpless and those who fight at their side. Almost by definition, the life of a Paladin is an adventuring life, for every Paladin lives on the front lines of the cosmic struggle against annihilation. Fighters are rare enough among the ranks of a world's armies, but even fewer people can claim the calling of a Paladin. When they do receive the call, these blessed folk turn from their former occupations and take up arms and magic. [!infobox-class|paladin wfull] Core Paladin Traits --- --- Primary Ability Strength and Charisma Hit Dice Die D10 per Paladin level Saving Throws Proficiencies Wisdom and Charisma Skill Proficiencies Choose 2: Athletics, Insight, Intimidation, Medicine, Persuasion, or Religion Weapon Proficiencies Simple and Martial weapons Tool Proficiencies None Armor Training Light, Medium, and Heavy armor and Shields Starting Equipment Choose A or B: (A) Chain Mail, Shield, Longsword, 6 Javelins, Holy Symbol, Priest’s Pack, and 9 GP; or (B) 150 GP [!class-divider|paladin small] [!class-header|paladin] Becoming a Paladin [!class-header|paladin] As a Level 1 Character Gain all the traits in the Core Paladin Traits table. Gain the Paladin’s level 1 features, which are listed in the Paladin Features table. [!class-header|paladin] As a Multiclass Character Gain the following traits from the Core Paladin Traits table: Hit Point Die, proficiency with Martial weapons, and training with Light and Medium armor and Shields. Gain the Paladin’s level 1 features, which are listed in the Paladin Features table. See the multiclassing rules to determine your available spell slots. [!class-divider|paladin small] [!class-header|paladin] Paladin Class Features As a Paladin, you gain the following class features when you reach the specified Paladin levels. These features are listed in the Paladin Features table. [!class-table|paladin] Level Prof. Bonus Class Features Channel Divinity Prepared Spells 1 2 3 4 5 1 +2 Lay On Hands, Spellcasting, Weapon Mastery — 2 2 — — — — 2 +2 Fighting Style, Paladin's Smite — 3 2 — — — — 3 +2 Channel Divinity, Paladin Subclass 2 4 3 — — — — 4 +2 Ability Score Improvement 2 5 3 — — — — 5 +3 Extra Attack, Faithful Steed 2 6 4 2 — — — 6 +3 Aura of Protection 2 6 4 2 — — — 7 +3 Subclass Feature 2 7 4 3 — — — 8 +3 Ability Score Improvement 2 7 4 3 — — — 9 +4 Abjure Foes 2 9 4 3 2 — — 10 +4 Aura of Courage 2 9 4 3 2 — — 11 +4 Radiant Strikes 3 10 4 3 3 — — 12 +4 Ability Score Improvement 3 10 4 3 3 — — 13 +5 — 3 11 4 3 3 1 — 14 +5 Restoring Touch 3 11 4 3 3 1 — 15 +5 Subclass Feature 3 12 4 3 3 2 — 16 +5 Ability Score Improvement 3 12 4 3 3 2 — 17 +6 — 3 14 4 3 3 3 1 18 +6 Aura Expansion 3 14 4 3 3 3 1 19 +6 Epic Boon 3 15 4 3 3 3 2 20 +6 Subclass Feature 3 15 4 3 3 3 2

title: Ranger Features
content: [!class-header|ranger] Ranger Feature List [!class-divider|ranger] [!button-pack|ranger] Ranger Summary Ranger Features Ranger Subclasses Ranger Spell List [!class-divider|ranger] [!class-level|ranger] Level 1 [!class-feature|ranger] Spellcasting Level 1 Ranger Feature You have learned to channel the magical essence of nature to cast spells. See the Player's Handbook for the rules on spellcasting. The information below details how you use those rules with Ranger spells. Spell Slots. The Ranger Features table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a Long Rest. Prepared Spells of Level 1+. You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose two level 1 Ranger spells. Cure Wounds and Ensnaring Strike are recommended. The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Ranger levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Ranger Features table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional Ranger spells until the number of spells on your list matches the number in the table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. For example, if you're a level 5 Ranger, your list of prepared spells can include six Ranger spells of level 1 or 2 in any combination. If another Ranger feature gives you spells that you always have prepared, those spells don't count against the number of spells you can prepare with this feature, but those spells otherwise count as Ranger spells for you. Changing Your Prepared Spells. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can replace one spell on your list with another Ranger spell for which you have spell slots. Spellcasting Ability. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for your Ranger spells. Spellcasting Focus. You can use a Druidic Focus as a Spellcasting Focus for your Ranger spells. [!class-divider|ranger small] [!class-feature|ranger] Favored Enemy Level 1 Ranger Feature You always have the Hunter's Mark spell prepared. You can cast it twice without expending a spell slot, and you regain all expended uses of this ability when you finish a Long Rest. The number of times you can cast the spell without a spell slot increases when you reach certain Ranger levels, as shown in the Favored Enemy column of the Ranger Features table. [!class-divider|ranger small] [!class-feature|ranger] Weapon Mastery Level 1 Ranger Feature Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency, such as Longbows and Shortswords. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change the kinds of weapons you chose. For example, you could switch to using the mastery properties of Scimitars and Longswords. [!class-level|ranger] Level 2 [!class-feature|ranger] Deft Explorer Level 2 Ranger Feature Thanks to your travels, you gain the following benefits: Expertise. Choose one of your skill proficiencies with which you lack Expertise. You gain Expertise in that skill. Languages. You know two languages of your choice. [!class-divider|ranger small] [!class-feature|ranger] Fighting Style Level 2 Ranger Feature You gain a Fighting Style feat of your choice. Instead of choosing one of those feats, you can choose the option below. Druidic Warrior. You learn two Druid cantrips of your choice. Guidance and Starry Wisp are recommended. The chosen cantrips count as Ranger spells for you, and Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for them. Whenever you gain a Ranger level, you can replace one of these cantrips with another Druid cantrip. [!class-level|ranger] Level 3 [!class-feature|ranger] Ranger Subclass Level 3 Ranger Feature You gain a Ranger subclass of your choice. The following Ranger subclasses are available: Beast Master Gloom Stalker Hunter Fey Wanderer Winter Walker [!class-level|ranger] Level 4 [!class-feature|ranger] Ability Score Improvement Level 4 Ranger Feature You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Ranger levels 8, 12, and 16. [!class-level|ranger] Level 5 [!class-feature|ranger] Extra Attack Level 5 Ranger Feature You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn. [!class-level|ranger] Level 6 [!class-feature|ranger] Roving Level 6 Ranger Feature Your Speed increases by 10 feet while you aren't wearing Heavy armor. You also have a Climb Speed and a Swim Speed equal to your Speed. [!class-level|ranger] Level 9 [!class-feature|ranger] Expertise Level 9 Ranger Feature Choose two of your skill proficiencies with which you lack Expertise. You gain Expertise in those skills. [!class-level|ranger] Level 10 [!class-feature|ranger] Tireless Level 10 Ranger Feature Primal forces now help fuel you on your journeys, granting you the following benefits: Temporary Hit Points. As a Magic action, you can give yourself a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to 1d8 plus your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1). You can use this action a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. Decrease Exhaustion. Whenever you finish a Short Rest, your Exhaustion level, if any, decreases by 1. [!class-level|ranger] Level 13 [!class-feature|ranger] Relentless Hunter Level 13 Ranger Feature Taking damage can't break your Concentration on Hunter's Mark. [!class-level|ranger] Level 14 [!class-feature|ranger] Nature's Veil Level 14 Ranger Feature You invoke spirits of nature to magically hide yourself. As a Bonus Action, you can give yourself the Invisible condition until the end of your next turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. [!class-level|ranger] Level 17 [!class-feature|ranger] Precise Hunter Level 17 Ranger Feature You have Advantage on attack rolls against the creature currently marked by your Hunter's Mark. [!class-level|ranger] Level 18 [!class-feature|ranger] Feral Senses Level 18 Ranger Feature Your connection to the forces of nature grants you Blindsight with a range of 30 feet. [!class-level|ranger] Level 19 [!class-feature|ranger] Epic Boon Level 19 Ranger Feature You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. Boon of Dimensional Travel is recommended. [!class-level|ranger] Level 20 [!class-feature|ranger] Foe Slayer Level 20 Ranger Feature The damage die of your Hunter's Mark becomes a d10 instead of a d6.
