title: Beast Master
content: [!class-header|ranger] Beast Master [!class-divider|ranger] [!button-pack|ranger] Ranger Ranger Features Ranger Subclasses Ranger Spell List [!class-divider|ranger] !BeastMaster_Banner.png [!class-recite|ranger] Bond with a Primal Beast A Beast Master forms a mystical bond with a special animal, drawing on primal magic and a deep connection to the natural world. Whether prowling through dense forests, soaring across open skies, or hunting beneath crashing waves, a Beast Master's companion serves as a loyal ally and trusted friend. [!class-level|ranger] Level 3 [!class-feature|ranger] Primal Companion Level 3 Beast Master Feature You magically summon a primal beast that draws strength from your bond with nature. Choose one of the following stat blocks: Beast of the Land Beast of the Sea Beast of the Sky You also determine the beast's appearance, choosing an appropriate animal form for the selected stat block. The beast is Friendly to you and your allies and obeys your commands. It vanishes if you die. The Beast in Combat. In combat, the beast acts during your turn. It can move and use its Reaction on its own, but the only action it takes is Dodge unless you: Use a Bonus Action to command it to take an action from its stat block or another action. Sacrifice one of your attacks when taking the Attack action to command it to take the Beast's Strike action. If you have the Incapacitated condition, the beast acts independently and is no longer limited to Dodge. Restoring or Replacing the Beast. If the beast died within the last hour, you can take a Magic action, touch it, and expend a spell slot. After 1 minute, the beast returns to life with all Hit Points restored. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you may summon a different primal beast in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you. If you already have a beast from this feature, the old one vanishes when the new one appears. [!class-divider|ranger small] [!class-feature|ranger] Beast of the Land Primal Companion Option [!class-table|ranger] Attribute Details Size Medium Beast Alignment Neutral AC 13 + your Wisdom modifier HP 5 + five times your Ranger level Hit Dice d8s equal to your Ranger level Speed 40 ft., Climb 40 ft. [!class-table|ranger] STR DEX CON INT WIS CHA 14 (+2) 14 (+2) 15 (+2) 8 (-1) 14 (+2) 11 (+0) [!class-table|ranger] Senses Languages CR Darkvision 60 ft., Passive Perception 12 Understands the languages you know None (XP 0; PB equals your Proficiency Bonus) Primal Bond. Add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check or saving throw the beast makes. Beast's Strike. Melee Attack Roll: Bonus equals your spell attack modifier, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1d8 + 2 + your Wisdom modifier Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage (chosen when summoned). If the beast moved at least 20 feet straight toward the target before the hit: The target takes an extra 1d6 damage of the same type. A Large or smaller target gains the Prone condition. [!class-divider|ranger small] [!class-feature|ranger] Beast of the Sky Primal Companion Option [!class-table|ranger] Attribute Details Size Small Beast Alignment Neutral AC 13 + your Wisdom modifier HP 4 + four times your Ranger level Hit Dice d6s equal to your Ranger level Speed 10 ft., Fly 60 ft. [!class-table|ranger] STR DEX CON INT WIS CHA 6 (-2) 16 (+3) 13 (+1) 8 (-1) 14 (+2) 11 (+0) [!class-table|ranger] Senses Languages CR Darkvision 60 ft., Passive Perception 12 Understands the languages you know None (XP 0; PB equals your Proficiency Bonus) Flyby. The beast doesn't provoke Opportunity Attacks when it flies out of an enemy's reach. Primal Bond. Add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check or saving throw the beast makes. Beast's Strike. Melee Attack Roll: Bonus equals your spell attack modifier, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1d4 + 3 + your Wisdom modifier Slashing damage. [!class-divider|ranger small] [!class-feature|ranger] Beast of the Sea Primal Companion Option [!class-table|ranger] Attribute Details Size Medium Beast Alignment Neutral AC 13 + your Wisdom modifier HP 5 + five times your Ranger level Hit Dice d8s equal to your Ranger level Speed 5 ft., Swim 60 ft. [!class-table|ranger] STR DEX CON INT WIS CHA 14 (+2) 14 (+2) 15 (+2) 8 (-1) 14 (+2) 11 (+0) [!class-table|ranger] Senses Languages CR Darkvision 90 ft., Passive Perception 12 Understands the languages you know None (XP 0; PB equals your Proficiency Bonus) Amphibious. The beast can breathe air and water. Primal Bond. Add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check or saving throw the beast makes. Beast's Strike. Melee Attack Roll: Bonus equals your spell attack modifier, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1d6 + 2 + your Wisdom modifier Bludgeoning or Piercing damage (chosen when summoned). The target gains the Grappled condition (escape DC equals your spell save DC). [!class-level|ranger] Level 7 [!class-feature|ranger] Exceptional Training Level 7 Beast Master Feature When you take a Bonus Action to command your Primal Companion to take an action, you can also command it to take one of the following actions using its Bonus Action: Dash Disengage Dodge Help In addition, whenever the beast hits with an attack roll and deals damage, it can deal either: Its normal damage type, or Force damage [!class-level|ranger] Level 11 [!class-feature|ranger] Bestial Fury Level 11 Beast Master Feature When you command your Primal Companion to take the Beast's Strike action, it can make two Beast's Strike attacks instead of one. In addition, the first time each turn the beast hits a creature affected by your Hunter's Mark, it deals extra Force damage equal to the bonus damage granted by that spell. [!class-level|ranger] Level 15 [!class-feature|ranger] Share Spells Level 15 Beast Master Feature When you cast a spell targeting yourself, you can also affect your Primal Companion with that spell if the beast is within 30 feet of you.

title: Fey Wanderer
content: [!class-header|ranger] Fey Wanderer [!class-divider|ranger] [!button-pack|ranger] Ranger Ranger Features Ranger Subclasses Ranger Spell List [!class-divider|ranger] !FeyWanderer_Banner.png [!class-recite|ranger] Wield Fey Mirth and Fury A fey mystique surrounds you, thanks to the boon of an archfey or a location in the Feywild that transformed you. However you gained fey magic, you are now a Fey Wanderer. Your joyful laughter brightens the hearts of the downtrodden, while your martial prowess strikes terror into your foes. Great is the mirth of the fey, and dreadful is their fury. [!class-level|ranger] Level 3 [!class-feature|ranger] Dreadful Strikes Level 3 Fey Wanderer Feature You can augment your weapon strikes with mind-scarring magic drawn from the murky hollows of the Feywild. When you hit a creature with a weapon, you can deal an extra 1d4 Psychic damage to the target. A creature can take this extra damage only once per turn. The extra damage increases to 1d6 when you reach Ranger level 11. [!class-divider|ranger small] [!class-feature|ranger] Fey Wanderer Spells Level 3 Fey Wanderer Feature When you reach certain Ranger levels, you always have the following spells prepared. [!class-table|ranger] Ranger Level Prepared Spell 3 Charm Person 5 Misty Step 9 Summon Fey 13 Dimension Door 17 Mislead You also possess a fey blessing. Choose one from the table below or determine it randomly. [!class-table|ranger] 1d6 Feywild Gift 1 Illusory butterflies flutter around you whenever you take a Short or Long Rest. 2 Flowers bloom from your hair each dawn. 3 You faintly smell of cinnamon, lavender, nutmeg, or another comforting herb or spice. 4 Your shadow dances while no one is looking directly at it. 5 Horns or antlers sprout from your head. 6 Your skin and hair change color each dawn. [!class-divider|ranger small] [!class-feature|ranger] Otherworldly Glamour Level 3 Fey Wanderer Feature Whenever you make a Charisma check, you gain a bonus equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum bonus of +1). In addition, you gain proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: Deception Performance Persuasion [!class-level|ranger] Level 7 [!class-feature|ranger] Beguiling Twist Level 7 Fey Wanderer Feature The magic of the Feywild guards your mind. You have Advantage on saving throws made to avoid or end the Charmed or Frightened condition. In addition, whenever you or a creature you can see within 120 feet succeeds on a saving throw to avoid or end either of those conditions, you can use your Reaction to force a different creature you can see within 120 feet of yourself to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the target is either Charmed or Frightened (your choice) for 1 minute. The target repeats the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. [!class-level|ranger] Level 11 [!class-feature|ranger] Fey Reinforcements Level 11 Fey Wanderer Feature You can cast Summon Fey without requiring a Material component. In addition, you can cast the spell once without expending a spell slot. You regain the ability to do so when you finish a Long Rest. Whenever you cast the spell, you can modify it so that it doesn't require Concentration. If you do so, its duration becomes 1 minute for that casting. [!class-level|ranger] Level 15 [!class-feature|ranger] Misty Wanderer Level 15 Fey Wanderer Feature You can cast Misty Step without expending a spell slot. You can do so a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. In addition, whenever you cast Misty Step, you can bring along one willing creature you can see within 5 feet of yourself. That creature teleports to an unoccupied space of your choice within 5 feet of your destination.

title: Gloom Stalker
content: [!class-header|ranger] Gloom Stalker [!class-divider|ranger] [!button-pack|ranger] Ranger Ranger Features Ranger Subclasses Ranger Spell List [!class-divider|ranger] !GloomStalker_Banner.png [!class-recite|ranger] Draw on Shadow Magic to Fight Your Foes Gloom Stalkers are at home in the darkest places, wielding magic drawn from the Shadowfell to combat enemies that lurk in darkness. They stalk the edges of torchlight, strike from unseen positions, and vanish back into the shadows before their foes can react. [!class-level|ranger] Level 3 [!class-feature|ranger] Dread Ambusher Level 3 Gloom Stalker Feature You have mastered the art of creating fearsome ambushes, granting you the following benefits: Ambusher's Leap. At the start of your first turn of each combat, your Speed increases by 10 feet until the end of that turn. Dreadful Strike. When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can deal an extra 2d6 Psychic damage. You can use this benefit only once per turn. You can use it a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. Initiative Bonus. When you roll Initiative, you can add your Wisdom modifier to the roll. [!class-divider|ranger small] [!class-feature|ranger] Gloom Stalker Spells Level 3 Gloom Stalker Feature When you reach certain Ranger levels, you always have the following spells prepared. [!class-table|ranger] Ranger Level Prepared Spell 3 Disguise Self 5 Rope Trick 9 Fear 13 Greater Invisibility 17 Seeming [!class-divider|ranger small] [!class-feature|ranger] Umbral Sight Level 3 Gloom Stalker Feature You gain Darkvision with a range of 60 feet. If you already have Darkvision, its range increases by 60 feet. You are also adept at evading creatures that rely on Darkvision. While entirely within Darkness, you have the Invisible condition to any creature that relies on Darkvision to see you in that Darkness. [!class-level|ranger] Level 7 [!class-feature|ranger] Iron Mind Level 7 Gloom Stalker Feature You have honed your ability to resist mind-altering powers. You gain proficiency in Wisdom saving throws. If you already have this proficiency, you instead gain proficiency in either Intelligence or Charisma saving throws (your choice). [!class-level|ranger] Level 11 [!class-feature|ranger] Stalker's Flurry Level 11 Gloom Stalker Feature The Psychic damage of your Dreadful Strike increases from 2d6 to 2d8. In addition, whenever you use the Dreadful Strike effect of your Dread Ambusher feature, you can cause one of the following effects: Sudden Strike. You can make another attack with the same weapon against a different creature that is within 5 feet of the original target and within the weapon's range. Mass Fear. The target and each creature within 10 feet of it must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, a creature gains the Frightened condition until the start of your next turn. [!class-level|ranger] Level 15 [!class-feature|ranger] Shadowy Dodge Level 15 Gloom Stalker Feature When a creature makes an attack roll against you, you can take a Reaction to impose Disadvantage on that roll. Whether the attack hits or misses, you can then teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

title: Hollow Warden
content: [!class-header|ranger] Hollow Warden [!class-divider|ranger] [!button-pack|ranger] Ranger Ranger Features Ranger Subclasses Ranger Spell List [!class-divider|ranger] !HollowWarden_Banner.png [!class-recite|ranger] Draw on the Might of Ancient Wild Terrors Legends tell that the oldest and most bloodthirsty horrors lurk deep within the forgotten places of the world. Hollow Wardens revere these ancient terrors and draw power from them, transforming themselves into monstrous guardians of the wild. They stalk jagged coastlines, ancient forests, and lonely mountain crags, becoming living embodiments of nature’s most primal fears. [!class-level|ranger] Level 3 [!class-feature|ranger] Hollow Warden Spells Level 3 Hollow Warden Feature When you reach the specified Ranger levels, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared. [!class-table|ranger] Ranger Level Prepared Spells 3 Wrathful Smite 5 Alter Self 9 Phantom Steed 13 Dominate Beast 17 Steel Wind Strike [!class-divider|ranger small] [!class-feature|ranger] Wrath of the Wild Level 3 Hollow Warden Feature You draw power from the strange and ancient horrors of the wild. Bloody antlers, rotting fangs, twisted shadows, or other unsettling manifestations emerge around you. As a Bonus Action, you can expend a use of Favored Enemy to transform into a ghastly form. The transformation lasts for 1 minute or until you gain the Incapacitated condition, die, or end it early (no action required). While transformed, you gain the following benefits: Ancient Armor. You gain a +1 bonus to AC. This bonus increases to +2 when you reach Ranger level 11. Prowling Retribution. Immediately after a creature you can see within 5 feet of yourself deals damage to you or one of your allies, you can make an Opportunity Attack against that creature. Unnerving Aura. When you transform and at the start of each of your turns thereafter, each creature of your choice in a 10-foot Emanation originating from you must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature has the Frightened condition until the start of your next turn. [!class-level|ranger] Level 7 [!class-feature|ranger] Hungering Might Level 7 Hollow Warden Feature You gain a bonus to Constitution saving throws equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of +1). In addition, once per turn while transformed by Wrath of the Wild, when you hit a creature with an attack roll, you regain Hit Points equal to 1d10 plus your Wisdom modifier if you are Bloodied when the attack hits. [!class-level|ranger] Level 11 [!class-feature|ranger] Rot and Violence Level 11 Hollow Warden Feature Your devotion to ancient wild horrors further transforms your body and spirit. While transformed by Wrath of the Wild, you gain the following benefits: Menacing Aura. When a creature fails its saving throw against your Unnerving Aura, it also can't regain Hit Points or take Reactions until the start of your next turn. Strangling Roots. When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can activate the Sap or Slow Weapon Mastery property in addition to another mastery property you are using with that weapon. [!class-level|ranger] Level 15 [!class-feature|ranger] Ancient Might Level 15 Hollow Warden Feature You become fully suffused with the terrible power of the ancient wilds. You gain the following benefits: Ominous Strikes. When you hit a creature that has the Frightened condition with an attack roll, the attack deals extra damage equal to your Wisdom modifier. Persistent Wrath. If you are reduced to 0 Hit Points but not killed outright while transformed by Wrath of the Wild, your Hit Points instead become equal to twice your Ranger level. Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest. You can also restore a use by expending a level 4+ spell slot (no action required). Timeless. You gain Immunity to the Exhaustion condition.

title: Hunter
content: [!class-header|ranger] Hunter [!class-divider|ranger] [!button-pack|ranger] Ranger Ranger Features Ranger Subclasses Ranger Spell List [!class-divider|ranger] !Hunter_Banner.png [!class-recite|ranger] Protect Nature and People from Destruction Hunters stalk dangerous prey across the wilderness and beyond, using their skills and instincts to safeguard both civilization and the natural world. Whether facing marauding monsters, rampaging beasts, or supernatural threats, a Hunter learns how to adapt their tactics to overcome any foe. [!class-level|ranger] Level 3 [!class-feature|ranger] Hunter's Lore Level 3 Hunter Feature You can call on the forces of nature to reveal the strengths and weaknesses of your prey. While a creature is marked by your Hunter's Mark, you know whether that creature has any Immunities, Resistances, or Vulnerabilities. If it has any, you know exactly what they are. [!class-divider|ranger small] [!class-feature|ranger] Hunter's Prey Level 3 Hunter Feature You gain one of the following feature options of your choice. Whenever you finish a Short or Long Rest, you can replace the chosen option with the other one. Colossus Slayer. Your tenacity can wear down even the most resilient foes. Once per turn, when you hit a creature with a weapon and that creature is missing any of its Hit Points, the attack deals an extra 1d8 damage. Horde Breaker. Once on each of your turns when you make an attack with a weapon, you can make another attack with the same weapon against a different creature. The second target must: Be within 5 feet of the original target. Be within the weapon's range. Not have been attacked by you this turn. [!class-level|ranger] Level 7 [!class-feature|ranger] Defensive Tactics Level 7 Hunter Feature You gain one of the following feature options of your choice. Whenever you finish a Short or Long Rest, you can replace the chosen option with the other one. Escape the Horde. Opportunity Attacks against you have Disadvantage. Multiattack Defense. When a creature hits you with an attack roll, that creature has Disadvantage on all other attack rolls it makes against you until the end of the current turn. [!class-level|ranger] Level 11 [!class-feature|ranger] Superior Hunter's Prey Level 11 Hunter Feature Once per turn when you deal damage to a creature marked by your Hunter's Mark, you can also deal the spell's extra damage to a different creature you can see within 30 feet of the original target. [!class-level|ranger] Level 15 [!class-feature|ranger] Superior Hunter's Defense Level 15 Hunter Feature When you take damage, you can use your Reaction to gain Resistance to that damage type and any other damage of the same type until the end of the current turn.

title: Ranger subclasses
content: [!button-pack|ranger] Ranger Summary Ranger Features Ranger Subclasses Ranger Spell List [!class-header|ranger] Ranger Subclasses [!cards|3] Beast Master Fey Wanderer Gloom Stalker Hunter Winter Walker Hollow Warden

title: Winter Walker
content: [!class-header|ranger] Winter Walker [!class-divider|ranger] [!button-pack|ranger] Ranger Ranger Features Ranger Subclasses Ranger Spell List [!class-divider|ranger] !WinterWalker_Banner.png [!class-recite|ranger] Withstand the Horrors of the Frigid Wasteland Winter Walkers hone their craft in the bleak and frozen wilds of places such as Icewind Dale. Rimed with ice and hardened by endless winters, these ruthless Rangers hunt the monsters that stalk arctic wastes, eventually becoming chilling terrors themselves. More than any other Rangers of the frozen north, Winter Walkers are versed in the region’s strange phenomena, from the lingering magic of fallen Netherese cities to the threats lurking beneath the ice and deep underground. Some believe their powers are a curse bestowed by nature spirits. Others claim they are forever marked by exposure to ancient evils. Whatever the truth, Winter Walkers inspire equal measures of respect and fear. [!class-level|ranger] Level 3 [!class-feature|ranger] Frigid Explorer Level 3 Winter Walker Feature You gain the following benefits: Frost Resistance. You have Resistance to Cold damage. Polar Strikes. Once per turn when you hit a creature with a weapon, you can deal an extra 1d4 Cold damage to the target. This extra damage ignores Resistance to Cold damage. When you reach Ranger level 11, the extra damage increases to 1d6. [!class-divider|ranger small] [!class-feature|ranger] Hunter's Rime Level 3 Winter Walker Feature Ice rimes both you and your prey, protecting you while hindering your quarry. Whenever you cast Hunter's Mark, you gain Temporary Hit Points equal to 1d10 + your Ranger level. In addition, while a creature is marked by your Hunter's Mark, it can't take the Disengage action. [!class-divider|ranger small] [!class-feature|ranger] Winter Walker Spells Level 3 Winter Walker Feature Your connection to the frozen wilds grants you additional magic. When you reach certain Ranger levels, you always have the following spells prepared. [!class-table|ranger] Ranger Level Prepared Spells 3 Ice Knife 5 Pass without Trace 9 Remove Curse 13 Ice Storm 17 Cone of Cold [!class-level|ranger] Level 7 [!class-feature|ranger] Fortifying Soul Level 7 Winter Walker Feature Your experience surviving the harshest environments allows you to bolster your companions. When you finish a Short Rest, choose a number of creatures you can see equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of one). Each chosen creature: Regains Hit Points equal to 1d10 + your Ranger level. Gains Advantage on saving throws to avoid or end the Frightened condition for 1 hour. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest. [!class-level|ranger] Level 11 [!class-feature|ranger] Chilling Retribution Level 11 Winter Walker Feature When a creature hits you with an attack roll, you can take a Reaction to force it to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature gains the Frightened condition until the end of your next turn. While Frightened in this way, its Speed is reduced to 0. You can use this Reaction a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. [!class-level|ranger] Level 15 [!class-feature|ranger] Frozen Haunt Level 15 Winter Walker Feature When you cast Hunter's Mark, you can assume a ghostly, snow-laden form. This form lasts until the spell ends. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest unless you expend a level 4+ spell slot (no action required). While in this form, you gain the following benefits: Frozen Soul. You have Immunity to Cold damage. When you first assume this form and at the start of each of your turns thereafter, each creature of your choice in a 15-foot Emanation originating from you takes 2d4 Cold damage. Partially Incorporeal. You have Immunity to the Grappled, Prone, and Restrained conditions. You can move through creatures and objects as though they were Difficult Terrain. If you end your turn inside a creature or object, you take 1d10 Force damage. If this form ends while you are inside a creature or object, you are shunted to the nearest unoccupied space.

title: Ranger
content: [!class-header|ranger] Ranger [!class-divider|ranger] [!button-pack|ranger] Ranger Summary Ranger Features Ranger Subclasses Ranger Spell List [!class-divider|ranger] [!class-recite|ranger] A Wandering Warrior Imbued with Primal Magic Far from bustling cities, amid the trees of trackless forests and across wide plains, Rangers keep their unending watch in the wilderness. Rangers learn to track their quarry as a predator does, moving stealthily through the wilds and hiding themselves in brush and rubble. Thanks to their connection with nature, Rangers can also cast spells that harness primal powers of the wilderness. A Ranger’s talents and magic are honed with deadly focus to protect the world from the ravages of monsters and tyrants. [!infobox-class|ranger wfull] Core Ranger Traits --- --- Primary Ability Dexterity and Wisdom Hit Dice Die D10 per Ranger level Saving Throws Proficiencies Strength and Dexterity Skill Proficiencies Choose 3: Animal Handling, Athletics, Insight, Investigation, Nature, Perception, Stealth, or Survival Weapon Proficiencies Simple and Martial weapons Tool Proficiencies None Armor Training Light and Medium armor and Shields Starting Equipment Choose A or B: (A) Studded Leather Armor, Scimitar, Shortsword, Longbow, 20 Arrows, Quiver, Druidic Focus (sprig of mistletoe), Explorer’s Pack, and 7 GP; or (B) 150 GP [!class-divider|ranger small] [!class-header|ranger] Becoming a Ranger [!class-header|ranger] As a Level 1 Character Gain all the traits in the Core Ranger Traits table. Gain the Ranger's level 1 features, which are listed in the Ranger Features table. [!class-header|ranger] As a Multiclass Character Gain the following traits from the Core Ranger Traits table: Hit Point Die, proficiency with Martial weapons, proficiency in one skill of your choice from the Ranger's skill list, and training with Light and Medium armor and Shields. Gain the Ranger's level 1 features, which are listed in the Ranger Features table. See the multiclassing rules to determine your available spell slots. [!class-divider|ranger small] [!class-header|ranger] Ranger Class Features As a Ranger, you gain the following class features when you reach the specified Ranger levels. These features are listed in the Ranger Features table. [!class-table|ranger] Level Prof. Bonus Class Features Favored Enemy Prepared Spells 1 2 3 4 5 1 +2 Spellcasting, Favored Enemy, Weapon Mastery 2 2 2 — — — — 2 +2 Deft Explorer, Fighting Style 2 3 2 — — — — 3 +2 Ranger Subclass 2 4 3 — — — — 4 +2 Ability Score Improvement 2 5 3 — — — — 5 +3 Extra Attack 3 6 4 2 — — — 6 +3 Roving 3 6 4 2 — — — 7 +3 Subclass Feature 3 7 4 3 — — — 8 +3 Ability Score Improvement 3 7 4 3 — — — 9 +4 Expertise 4 9 4 3 2 — — 10 +4 Tireless 4 9 4 3 2 — — 11 +4 Subclass Feature 4 10 4 3 3 — — 12 +4 Ability Score Improvement 4 10 4 3 3 — — 13 +5 Relentless Hunter 5 11 4 3 3 1 — 14 +5 Nature's Veil 5 11 4 3 3 1 — 15 +5 Subclass Feature 5 12 4 3 3 2 — 16 +5 Ability Score Improvement 5 12 4 3 3 2 — 17 +6 Precise Hunter 6 14 4 3 3 3 1 18 +6 Feral Senses 6 14 4 3 3 3 1 19 +6 Epic Boon 6 15 4 3 3 3 2 20 +6 Foe Slayer 6 15 4 3 3 3 2

title: Rogue Features
content: [!class-header|rogue] Rogue Feature List [!class-divider|rogue] [!button-pack|rogue] Rogue Summary Rogue Features Rogue Subclasses [!class-divider|rogue] [!class-level|rogue] Level 1 [!class-feature|rogue] Expertise Level 1 Rogue Feature You gain Expertise in two of your skill proficiencies of your choice. Sleight of Hand and Stealth are recommended if you have proficiency in them. At Rogue level 6, you can choose two more skill proficiencies to gain Expertise in. [!class-divider|rogue small] [!class-feature|rogue] Sneak Attack Level 1 Rogue Feature You know how to strike subtly and exploit a foe's distraction. Once per turn, you can deal an extra 1d6 damage to one creature you hit with an attack if you have Advantage on the attack roll. The attack must use a Finesse or Ranged weapon. You don't need Advantage if: At least one ally is within 5 feet of the target The ally isn't Incapacitated You don't have Disadvantage on the attack roll The extra damage increases with Rogue level, as shown in the Sneak Attack column of the Rogue Features table. [!class-divider|rogue small] [!class-feature|rogue] Thieves' Cant Level 1 Rogue Feature You picked up various languages in the communities where you plied your roguish talents. You know Thieves' Cant and one other language of your choice. [!class-divider|rogue small] [!class-feature|rogue] Weapon Mastery Level 1 Rogue Feature Your training allows you to use the mastery properties of two weapons of your choice that you're proficient with, such as Daggers and Shortswords. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change the kinds of weapons you chose. [!class-level|rogue] Level 2 [!class-feature|rogue] Cunning Action Level 2 Rogue Feature Your quick thinking and agility allow you to move and act quickly. On your turn, you can take one of the following actions as a Bonus Action: Dash Disengage Hide [!class-level|rogue] Level 3 [!class-feature|rogue] Rogue Subclass Level 3 Rogue Feature You gain a Rogue subclass of your choice. The following Rogue subclasses are available: Thief Assassin Arcane Trickster Soulknife [!class-divider|rogue small] [!class-feature|rogue] Steady Aim Level 3 Rogue Feature As a Bonus Action, you give yourself Advantage on your next attack roll on the current turn. You can use this feature only if you haven't moved during this turn, and after you use it, your Speed becomes 0 until the end of the current turn. [!class-level|rogue] Level 4 [!class-feature|rogue] Ability Score Improvement Level 4 Rogue Feature You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Rogue levels 8, 12, and 16. [!class-level|rogue] Level 5 [!class-feature|rogue] Cunning Strike Level 5 Rogue Feature You've developed cunning ways to use your Sneak Attack. When you deal Sneak Attack damage, you can add one of the following effects: Poison (Cost: 1d6) Trip (Cost: 1d6) Withdraw (Cost: 1d6) [!class-divider|rogue small] [!class-feature|rogue] Uncanny Dodge Level 5 Rogue Feature When an attacker that you can see hits you with an attack roll, you can take a Reaction to halve the attack's damage against you. [!class-level|rogue] Level 6 [!class-feature|rogue] Expertise Level 6 Rogue Feature Choose two additional skill proficiencies. You gain Expertise in those skills. [!class-level|rogue] Level 7 [!class-feature|rogue] Evasion Level 7 Rogue Feature When you're subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage on a success and only half damage on a failure. You can't use this feature while Incapacitated. [!class-divider|rogue small] [!class-feature|rogue] Reliable Talent Level 7 Rogue Feature Whenever you make an ability check that uses one of your skill or tool proficiencies, you can treat a d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10. [!class-level|rogue] Level 10 [!class-feature|rogue] Improved Cunning Strike Level 11 Rogue Feature You can use up to two Cunning Strike effects when you deal Sneak Attack damage, paying the die cost for each effect. [!class-level|rogue] Level 13 [!class-feature|rogue] Devious Strikes Level 14 Rogue Feature You gain the following additional Cunning Strike options: Daze (Cost: 2d6) Knock Out (Cost: 6d6) Obscure (Cost: 3d6) [!class-level|rogue] Level 15 [!class-feature|rogue] Slippery Mind Level 15 Rogue Feature Your cunning mind is exceptionally difficult to control. You gain proficiency in Wisdom and Charisma saving throws. [!class-level|rogue] Level 18 [!class-feature|rogue] Elusive Level 18 Rogue Feature No attack roll can have Advantage against you unless you have the Incapacitated condition. [!class-level|rogue] Level 19 [!class-feature|rogue] Epic Boon Level 19 Rogue Feature You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. Boon of the Night Spirit is recommended. [!class-level|rogue] Level 20 [!class-feature|rogue] Stroke of Luck Level 20 Rogue Feature If you fail a D20 Test, you can turn the roll into a 20. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

title: Phantom
content: [!class-header|rogue] Phantom [!class-divider|rogue] [!button-pack|rogue] Rogue Rogue Features Rogue Subclasses [!class-divider|rogue] !Phantom_Banner.png [!class-recite|rogue] Embrace Death and Wield Ghostly Power Some Rogues traverse the veil between life and death, shepherding opponents to the grave and slipping through the world as silently as a spirit. Their connection to death infuses their strikes with necrotic power and allows them to steal knowledge from departed souls. Thieves’ guilds and spies prize Phantoms for their uncanny ability to gather secrets from both the living and the dead. [!class-level|rogue] Level 3 [!class-feature|rogue] Wails from the Grave Level 3 Phantom Feature Immediately after you deal Sneak Attack damage to a creature on your turn, you can target a second creature you can see within 30 feet of the first creature. Roll a number of Sneak Attack dice equal to half your Sneak Attack dice for your level (round up). The second creature takes Necrotic damage equal to the total rolled as spectral wails echo around it. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Dexterity modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. [!class-divider|rogue small] [!class-feature|rogue] Whispers of the Dead Level 3 Phantom Feature Whenever you finish a Short or Long Rest, you can choose one skill or tool proficiency that you lack and gain proficiency with it. A ghostly presence shares its knowledge with you. You lose this proficiency when you use this feature again to choose a different one. [!class-level|rogue] Level 9 [!class-feature|rogue] Tokens of the Departed Level 9 Phantom Feature The spirits of the dead are drawn to you, manifesting as strange soul trinkets infused with lingering echoes of life. You gain two soul trinkets. A soul trinket is a Tiny object whose appearance is determined by you or the DM. If you move more than 30 feet from a trinket, it immediately teleports back to your person. You can use soul trinkets in the following ways: Death’s Knell. When you deal Sneak Attack damage, you can destroy one soul trinket and immediately use Wails from the Grave without expending a use of that feature. Life Essence. While you possess at least one soul trinket, you have Advantage on Death Saving Throws and Constitution saving throws. Spirit Query. As a Magic action, you can destroy one soul trinket to cast Augury without spell components, using Constitution as your spellcasting ability. If you know the creature associated with the trinket, you may instead ask its spirit one question. The spirit appears and answers as concisely as possible in a language it knew in life. Gaining Additional Soul Trinkets. When a creature you can see within 30 feet of you dies, you can take a Reaction to gain a new soul trinket. You can have a maximum of two soul trinkets at a time. If you gain another while at maximum capacity, one existing trinket is destroyed and replaced. The maximum number of trinkets increases to three at Rogue level 13 and four at Rogue level 17. Whenever you finish a Long Rest with fewer than two soul trinkets, you gain enough to return to two. [!class-divider|rogue small] [!class-feature|rogue] Voice of Death Level 9 Phantom Feature You can cast Speak with Dead once without expending a spell slot. The spell requires no material components and uses Dexterity as its spellcasting ability. You regain the ability to cast it in this way when you finish a Short or Long Rest. When you cast the spell, you can target one of your soul trinkets instead of a corpse, allowing the associated spirit to answer your questions. [!class-level|rogue] Level 13 [!class-feature|rogue] Ghost Walk Level 13 Phantom Feature As a Bonus Action, you assume a spectral form for 10 minutes. The effect ends early if you dismiss it (no action required). Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest unless you destroy one soul trinket from Tokens of the Departed to restore its use. While in this form, you gain the following benefits: Flight. You gain a Fly Speed of 10 feet and can hover. Hazy Form. Attack rolls against you have Disadvantage. Incorporeal Movement. You can move through creatures and objects as though they were Difficult Terrain. If you end your turn inside a creature or object, you take 1d10 Force damage. [!class-level|rogue] Level 17 [!class-feature|rogue] Death’s Friend Level 17 Phantom Feature Your bond with death grows stronger, granting the following benefits: Death’s Lament. When you use Wails from the Grave, you can deal the feature's Necrotic damage to both the original target and the secondary target. Draw of Death. When you roll Initiative, if you have no remaining soul trinkets, you immediately gain one soul trinket for your Tokens of the Departed feature.

title: Rogue Subclasses
content: [!class-header|rogue] Rogue Subclasses [!class-divider|rogue] [!button-pack|rogue] Rogue Summary Rogue Features Rogue Subclasses [!class-divider|rogue] [!cards|3] Arcane Trickster Assassin Soulknife Thief Phantom Scion of the Three

title: Scion of the Three
content: [!class-header|rogue] Scion of the Three [!class-divider|rogue] [!button-pack|rogue] Rogue Rogue Features Rogue Subclasses [!class-divider|rogue] !ScionoftheThree_Banner.png [!class-recite|rogue] Become a Gruesome Agent of Malice A Scion of the Three draws power from the Dead Three: Bane, god of tyranny Bhaal, god of murder Myrkul, god of death Whether through devotion, curse, or dark fate, these Rogues wield sinister gifts and strike fear into their enemies with supernatural brutality. [!class-level|rogue] Level 3 [!class-feature|rogue] Bloodthirst Level 3 Scion of the Three Feature When an enemy you can see within 30 feet of yourself takes damage and becomes Bloodied from that damage without being reduced to 0 Hit Points, you can take a Reaction to teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 5 feet of that creature. You can then make one melee attack against it. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. [!class-divider|rogue small] [!class-feature|rogue] Dread Allegiance Level 3 Scion of the Three Feature Choose one of the Dead Three: Bane, Bhaal, or Myrkul. You gain the associated damage Resistance and cantrip listed below. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for this cantrip. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change your choice. God Damage Resistance Cantrip Bane Psychic Minor Illusion Bhaal Poison Blade Ward Myrkul Necrotic Chill Touch [!class-level|rogue] Level 9 [!class-feature|rogue] Strike Fear Level 9 Scion of the Three Feature You gain the following Cunning Strike option: Terrify (Cost: 1d6). The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or gain the Frightened condition for 1 minute. While the target is Frightened in this way, you have Advantage on attack rolls against it. The target repeats the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. [!class-level|rogue] Level 13 [!class-feature|rogue] Aura of Malevolence Level 13 Scion of the Three Feature You radiate malignant power associated with your chosen allegiance. Whenever you use Bloodthirst and teleport, each creature of your choice within 10 feet of either: the space you left, or the space you teleport to (your choice) takes damage equal to your Intelligence modifier. The damage type matches the Resistance granted by your Dread Allegiance feature. Damage dealt by this feature ignores Resistance. [!class-level|rogue] Level 17 [!class-feature|rogue] Dread Incarnate Level 17 Scion of the Three Feature You gain the following benefits: Cutthroat. When you finish a Short Rest, you regain one expended use of Bloodthirst. Murderous Intent. When you roll Sneak Attack damage, any Sneak Attack die that rolls a 1 or 2 is treated as a 3.

title: Rogue
content: [!class-header|rogue] Rogue [!class-divider|rogue] [!button-pack|rogue] Rogue Summary Rogue Features Rogue Subclasses [!class-divider|rogue] [!class-recite|rogue] A Dexterous Expert in Stealth and Subterfuge Rogues rely on cunning, stealth, and their foes’ vulnerabilities to gain the upper hand in any situation. They have a knack for finding solutions where others see only obstacles. Many Rogues focus on stealth and deception, while others refine talents useful in dangerous environments, such as climbing, disarming traps, opening locks, and uncovering hidden secrets. In combat, Rogues favor precision over brute force. They would rather strike once at exactly the right moment than overwhelm an enemy with a flurry of attacks. Some Rogues begin their careers as criminals, while others use their talents to hunt criminals. Whatever their relationship with the law, few can match the subtle brilliance of a master Rogue. [!infobox-class|rogue wfull] Core Rogue Traits --- --- Primary Ability Dexterity Hit Dice Die D8 per Rogue level Saving Throws Proficiencies Dexterity and Intelligence Skill Proficiencies Choose 4: Acrobatics, Athletics, Deception, Insight, Intimidation, Investigation, Perception, Persuasion, Sleight of Hand, or Stealth Weapon Proficiencies Simple weapons and Martial weapons that have the Finesse or Light property Tool Proficiencies Thieves' Tools Armor Training Light armor Starting Equipment Choose A or B: (A) Leather Armor, 2 Daggers, Shortsword, Shortbow, 20 Arrows, Quiver, Thieves' Tools, Burglar's Pack, and 8 GP; or (B) 100 GP [!class-divider|rogue small] [!class-header|rogue] Becoming a Rogue [!class-header|rogue] As a Level 1 Character Gain all the traits in the Core Rogue Traits table. Gain the Rogue’s level 1 features, which are listed in the Rogue Features table. [!class-header|rogue] As a Multiclass Character Gain the following traits from the Core Rogue Traits table: Hit Point Die, proficiency in one skill of your choice from the Rogue skill list, proficiency with Thieves' Tools, and training with Light armor. Gain the Rogue’s level 1 features, which are listed in the Rogue Features table. [!class-divider|rogue small] [!class-header|rogue] Rogue Class Features As a Rogue, you gain the following class features when you reach the specified Rogue levels. These features are listed in the Rogue Features table. [!class-table|rogue] Level Prof. Bonus Class Features Sneak Attack 1 +2 Expertise, Sneak Attack, Thieves' Cant, Weapon Mastery 1d6 2 +2 Cunning Action 1d6 3 +2 Rogue Subclass, Steady Aim 2d6 4 +2 Ability Score Improvement 2d6 5 +3 Cunning Strike, Uncanny Dodge 3d6 6 +3 Expertise 3d6 7 +3 Evasion, Reliable Talent 4d6 8 +3 Ability Score Improvement 4d6 9 +4 Subclass Feature 5d6 10 +4 Ability Score Improvement 5d6 11 +4 Improved Cunning Strike 6d6 12 +4 Ability Score Improvement 6d6 13 +5 Subclass Feature 7d6 14 +5 Devious Strikes 7d6 15 +5 Slippery Mind 8d6 16 +5 Ability Score Improvement 8d6 17 +6 Subclass Feature 9d6 18 +6 Elusive 9d6 19 +6 Epic Boon 10d6 20 +6 Stroke of Luck 10d6

title: Sorcerer Features
content: [!class-header|sorcerer] Sorcerer Feature List [!class-divider|sorcerer] [!button-pack|sorcerer] Sorcerer Summary Sorcerer Features Sorcerer Subclasses Sorcerer Spell List Metamagic Options [!class-divider|sorcerer] [!class-level|sorcerer] Level 1 [!class-feature|sorcerer] Spellcasting Level 1 Sorcerer Feature Drawing from your innate magic, you can cast spells. See the Player’s Handbook for the rules on spellcasting. The information below details how you use those rules with Sorcerer spells. Cantrips. You know four Sorcerer cantrips of your choice. Light, Prestidigitation, Shocking Grasp, and Sorcerous Burst are recommended. Whenever you gain a Sorcerer level, you can replace one of your cantrips from this feature with another Sorcerer cantrip of your choice. When you reach Sorcerer levels 4 and 10, you learn another Sorcerer cantrip of your choice. Spell Slots. The Sorcerer Features table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended slots when you finish a Long Rest. Prepared Spells of Level 1+. You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose two level 1 Sorcerer spells. Burning Hands and Detect Magic are recommended. The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Sorcerer levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Sorcerer Features table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional Sorcerer spells until the number of spells on your list matches the number in the table. If another Sorcerer feature gives you spells that you always have prepared, those spells don’t count against the number of spells you can prepare with this feature. Changing Your Prepared Spells. Whenever you gain a Sorcerer level, you can replace one spell on your list with another Sorcerer spell for which you have spell slots. Spellcasting Ability. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for your Sorcerer spells. Spellcasting Focus. You can use an Arcane Focus as a Spellcasting Focus for your Sorcerer spells. [!class-divider|sorcerer small] [!class-feature|sorcerer] Innate Sorcery Level 1 Sorcerer Feature An event in your past left an indelible mark on you, infusing you with simmering magic. As a Bonus Action, you can unleash that magic for 1 minute. During that time: The spell save DC of your Sorcerer spells increases by 1. You have Advantage on attack rolls made with Sorcerer spells. You can use this feature twice, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. [!class-level|sorcerer] Level 2 [!class-feature|sorcerer] Font of Magic Level 2 Sorcerer Feature You gain Sorcery Points, which fuel certain Sorcerer features. Your number of Sorcery Points is shown in the Sorcery Points column of the Sorcerer Features table. You regain all expended Sorcery Points when you finish a Long Rest. [!class-divider|sorcerer small] [!class-feature|sorcerer] Metamagic Level 2 Sorcerer Feature Your magic flows from within, allowing you to alter spells to suit your needs. You gain two Metamagic options of your choice from the Metamagic Options list. To use a Metamagic option, you spend the required number of Sorcery Points. You can use only one Metamagic option on a spell unless the option specifically states otherwise. Whenever you gain a Sorcerer level, you can replace one Metamagic option you know with another. You gain two additional Metamagic options at Sorcerer levels 10 and 17. [!class-level|sorcerer] Level 3 [!class-feature|sorcerer] Sorcerer Subclass Level 3 Sorcerer Feature You gain a Sorcerer subclass of your choice. You can choose from the following Sorcerer Subclasses: Aberrant Sorcery Clockwork Sorcery Draconic Sorcery Wild Magic Sorcery [!class-level|sorcerer] Level 4 [!class-feature|sorcerer] Ability Score Improvement Level 4 Sorcerer Feature You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Sorcerer levels 8, 12, and 16. [!class-level|sorcerer] Level 5 [!class-feature|sorcerer] Sorcerous Restoration Level 5 Sorcerer Feature When you finish a Short Rest, you can regain expended Sorcery Points equal to up to half your Sorcerer level (round down). Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest. [!class-level|sorcerer] Level 7 [!class-feature|sorcerer] Sorcery Incarnate Level 7 Sorcerer Feature If you have no uses of Innate Sorcery remaining, you can activate it by spending 2 Sorcery Points when taking the Bonus Action to activate the feature. In addition, while Innate Sorcery is active, you can use up to two Metamagic options on each spell you cast. [!class-level|sorcerer] Level 19 [!class-feature|sorcerer] Epic Boon Level 19 Sorcerer Feature You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. Boon of Dimensional Travel is recommended. [!class-level|sorcerer] Level 20 [!class-feature|sorcerer] Arcane Apotheosis Level 20 Sorcerer Feature While your Innate Sorcery feature is active, you can use one Metamagic option on each of your turns without spending Sorcery Points on it.

title: Aberrant Sorcery
content: [!class-header|sorcerer] Aberrant Sorcery [!class-divider|sorcerer] [!button-pack|sorcerer] Sorcerer Sorcerer Features Sorcerer Subclasses Sorcerer Spell List Metamagic Options [!class-divider|sorcerer] !AberrantSorcery_Banner.png [!class-recite|sorcerer] Wield Unnatural Psionic Power An alien influence has wrapped its tendrils around your mind, giving you psionic power. You can now touch other minds with that power and alter the world around you. Will this power shine from you as a hopeful beacon to others, or will you be a terror to those who feel the stab of your mind? Perhaps a psychic wind from the Astral Plane carried psionic energy to you, or you were exposed to the Far Realm’s warping influence. Alternatively, you were implanted with a mind flayer tadpole, but your transformation into a mind flayer never occurred. Now the tadpole’s psionic power is yours. However you acquired this power, your mind is aflame with it. [!class-level|sorcerer] Level 3 [!class-feature|sorcerer] Psionic Spells Level 3 Aberrant Sorcery Feature When you reach a Sorcerer level specified in the Psionic Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared. [!class-table|sorcerer] Sorcerer Level Spells 3 Arms of Hadar, Calm Emotions, Detect Thoughts, Dissonant Whispers, Mind Sliver 5 Hunger of Hadar, Sending 7 Evard's Black Tentacles, Summon Aberration 9 Rary's Telepathic Bond, Telekinesis [!class-divider|sorcerer small] [!class-feature|sorcerer] Telepathic Speech Level 3 Aberrant Sorcery Feature You can form a telepathic connection between your mind and the mind of another. As a Bonus Action, choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. You and the chosen creature can communicate telepathically with each other while the two of you are within a number of miles of each other equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1 mile). To understand each other, you each must mentally use a language the other knows. The telepathic connection lasts for a number of minutes equal to your Sorcerer level. It ends early if you use this ability to form a connection with a different creature. [!class-level|sorcerer] Level 6 [!class-feature|sorcerer] Psionic Sorcery Level 6 Aberrant Sorcery Feature When you cast any level 1+ spell from your Psionic Spells feature, you can cast it by expending a spell slot as normal or by spending a number of Sorcery Points equal to the spell’s level. If you cast the spell using Sorcery Points, it requires no Verbal or Somatic components, and it requires no Material components unless they are consumed by the spell or have a cost specified in it. [!class-divider|sorcerer small] [!class-feature|sorcerer] Psychic Defenses Level 6 Aberrant Sorcery Feature You have Resistance to Psychic damage. In addition, you have Advantage on saving throws to avoid or end the Charmed or Frightened condition. [!class-level|sorcerer] Level 14 [!class-feature|sorcerer] Revelation in Flesh Level 14 Aberrant Sorcery Feature You can unleash the aberrant truth hidden within yourself. As a Bonus Action, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point or more to magically alter your body for 10 minutes. For each Sorcery Point you spend, you gain one of the following benefits of your choice, the effects of which last until the alteration ends. Aquatic Adaptation. You gain a Swim Speed equal to twice your Speed, and you can breathe underwater. Gills grow from your neck or flare behind your ears, and your fingers become webbed or you grow wriggling cilia. Glistening Flight. You gain a Fly Speed equal to your Speed, and you can hover. As you fly, your skin glistens with mucus or otherworldly light. See the Invisible. You can see any Invisible creature within 60 feet of yourself that isn't behind Total Cover. Your eyes also turn black or become writhing sensory tendrils. Wormlike Movement. Your body, along with any equipment you are wearing or carrying, becomes slimy and pliable. You can move through any space as narrow as 1 inch, and you can spend 5 feet of movement to escape from nonmagical restraints or the Grappled condition. [!class-level|sorcerer] Level 18 [!class-feature|sorcerer] Warping Implosion Level 18 Aberrant Sorcery Feature You can unleash a space-warping anomaly. As a Magic action, you teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 120 feet of yourself. Immediately after you disappear, each creature within 30 feet of the space you left must make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, a creature takes 3d10 Force damage and is pulled straight toward the space you left, ending in an unoccupied space as close to your former space as possible. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage only. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest unless you spend 5 Sorcery Points (no action required) to restore your use of it.

title: Draconic Sorcery
content: [!class-header|sorcerer] Draconic Sorcery [!class-divider|sorcerer] [!button-pack|sorcerer] Sorcerer Sorcerer Features Sorcerer Subclasses Sorcerer Spell List Metamagic Options [!class-divider|sorcerer] !DraconicSorcery_Banner.png [!class-recite|sorcerer] Breathe the Magic of Dragons Your innate magic comes from the gift of a dragon. Perhaps an ancient dragon facing death bequeathed some of its magical power to you or your ancestor. You might have absorbed magic from a site infused with draconic power, handled a relic from a dragon's hoard, or even carry draconic blood within your lineage. Whatever its source, draconic magic courses through your body and soul. [!class-level|sorcerer] Level 3 [!class-feature|sorcerer] Draconic Resilience Level 3 Draconic Sorcery Feature The magic in your body manifests physical traits of your draconic gift. Your Hit Point maximum increases by 3, and it increases by 1 whenever you gain another Sorcerer level. Parts of your body are also covered by dragon-like scales. While you aren't wearing armor, your base Armor Class equals 10 plus your Dexterity modifier and your Charisma modifier. [!class-divider|sorcerer small] [!class-feature|sorcerer] Draconic Spells Level 3 Draconic Sorcery Feature When you reach a Sorcerer level specified in the Draconic Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared. [!class-table|sorcerer] Sorcerer Level Spells 3 Alter Self, Chromatic Orb, Command, Dragon's Breath 5 Fear, Fly 7 Arcane Eye, Charm Monster 9 Legend Lore, Summon Dragon [!class-level|sorcerer] Level 6 [!class-feature|sorcerer] Elemental Affinity Level 6 Draconic Sorcery Feature Your draconic magic has an affinity with a damage type associated with dragons. Choose one of the following damage types: Acid Cold Fire Lightning Poison You gain Resistance to the chosen damage type. In addition, whenever you cast a spell that deals damage of that type, you can add your Charisma modifier to one damage roll of that spell. [!class-level|sorcerer] Level 14 [!class-feature|sorcerer] Dragon Wings Level 14 Draconic Sorcery Feature As a Bonus Action, you can cause draconic wings to appear on your back. The wings last for 1 hour or until you dismiss them (no action required). While they are present, you have a Fly Speed of 60 feet. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest unless you spend 3 Sorcery Points (no action required) to restore your use of it. [!class-level|sorcerer] Level 18 [!class-feature|sorcerer] Dragon Companion Level 18 Draconic Sorcery Feature You can cast Summon Dragon without a Material component. You can also cast the spell once without expending a spell slot, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a Long Rest. Whenever you begin casting the spell, you can modify it so that it doesn't require Concentration. If you do so, the spell's duration becomes 1 minute for that casting.

title: Shadow Sorcery
content: [!class-header|sorcerer] Shadow Sorcery [!class-divider|sorcerer] [!button-pack|sorcerer] Sorcerer Sorcerer Features Sorcerer Subclasses Sorcerer Spell List Metamagic Options [!class-divider|sorcerer] !ShadowSorcery_Banner.png [!class-recite|sorcerer] Bend Doom and Darkness to Your Will Your innate magic stems from the mysterious and sinister powers of the Shadowfell or other realms of supernatural darkness. Perhaps your bloodline traces back to a shadowy entity, or perhaps you were forever changed by exposure to the essence of a shadow dragon. Whatever its source, your magic allows you to command darkness, undeath, and ill omens. [!class-level|sorcerer] Level 3 [!class-feature|sorcerer] Shadow Spells Level 3 Shadow Sorcery Feature When you reach certain Sorcerer levels, you always have the following spells prepared. [!class-table|sorcerer] Sorcerer Level Prepared Spells 3 Bane, Darkness, Inflict Wounds, Pass Without Trace 5 Hunger of Hadar, Nondetection 7 Greater Invisibility, Phantasmal Killer 9 Contagion, Creation [!class-divider|sorcerer small] [!class-feature|sorcerer] Power of Shadow Level 3 Shadow Sorcery Feature You gain the following benefits: Eyes of the Dark. You have Darkvision with a range of 120 feet and Blindsight with a range of 10 feet. In addition, if a spell you cast creates an area of Darkness, you can see normally through that spell's darkness. Strength of the Grave. If you would drop to 0 Hit Points and not die outright, you can make a Charisma saving throw with a DC equal to 5 plus the damage taken. On a successful save, your Hit Points instead become a number equal to your Charisma modifier plus your Sorcerer level. After succeeding on this save, you can't use this benefit again until you finish a Long Rest. [!class-level|sorcerer] Level 6 [!class-feature|sorcerer] Beasts of Ill Omen Level 6 Shadow Sorcery Feature You can call forth a howling creature of shadow to hound your enemies. You can spend 3 Sorcery Points to cast Summon Beast as a Bonus Action without expending a spell slot, without preparing the spell, and without Material components. The summoned creature appears as a beast made of shadow. Enemies within 5 feet of the creature have Disadvantage on saving throws against spells you cast. Whenever you cast the spell, you can modify it so that it doesn't require Concentration. If you do so, the spell's duration becomes 1 minute for that casting, and it ends early if you cast the spell again. [!class-level|sorcerer] Level 14 [!class-feature|sorcerer] Shadow Walk Level 14 Shadow Sorcery Feature While you are in Dim Light or Darkness, you can take a Bonus Action to teleport up to 120 feet to an unoccupied space you can see that is also in Dim Light or Darkness. [!class-level|sorcerer] Level 18 [!class-feature|sorcerer] Umbral Form Level 18 Shadow Sorcery Feature When you use Innate Sorcery, you can also adopt a shadowy form. This form lasts while your Innate Sorcery remains active or until you end it (no action required). Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest unless you spend 6 Sorcery Points (no action required) to restore its use. While in this form, you gain the following benefits: Incorporeal Movement. You can move through creatures and objects as though they were Difficult Terrain. If you end your turn inside a creature or object, you take 1d10 Force damage. Shadow Resilience. You have Resistance to all damage except Force and Radiant damage.

title: Sorcerer Subclasses
content: [!class-header|sorcerer] Sorcerer Subclasses [!class-divider|sorcerer] [!button-pack|sorcerer] Sorcerer Summary Sorcerer Features Sorcerer Subclasses Sorcerer Spell List Metamagic Options [!class-divider|sorcerer] [!cards|3] Aberrant Sorcery Clockwork Sorcery Draconic Sorcery Wild Magic Sorcery Spellfire Sorcery Shadow Sorcery

title: Spellfire Sorcery
content: [!class-header|sorcerer] Spellfire Sorcery [!class-divider|sorcerer] [!button-pack|sorcerer] Sorcerer Sorcerer Features Sorcerer Subclasses Sorcerer Spell List Metamagic Options [!class-divider|sorcerer] !SpellfireSorcery_Banner.png [!class-recite|sorcerer] Wield Raw Magic Your innate power stems from the source of magic itself: the Weave. This connection manifests as a rare ability known as spellfire, and you surge with radiant bursts of raw magical energy. Your talent with spellfire allows you to heal allies, sear enemies, and absorb powerful spells. Wielders of spellfire often wander the world in pursuit of knowledge, adventure, and mastery over their remarkable gift. [!class-level|sorcerer] Level 3 [!class-feature|sorcerer] Spellfire Burst Level 3 Spellfire Sorcery Feature When you spend at least 1 Sorcery Point as part of a Magic action or a Bonus Action on your turn, you can unleash one of the following magical effects of your choice. You can do so only once per turn. Bolstering Flames. You or one creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself gains Temporary Hit Points equal to 1d4 plus your Charisma modifier. Radiant Fire. One creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself takes 1d4 Fire or Radiant damage (your choice). [!class-divider|sorcerer small] [!class-feature|sorcerer] Spellfire Spells Level 3 Spellfire Sorcery Feature When you reach a Sorcerer level specified in the Spellfire Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared. [!class-table|sorcerer] Sorcerer Level Spells 3 Cure Wounds, Guiding Bolt, Lesser Restoration, Scorching Ray 5 Aura of Vitality, Dispel Magic 7 Fire Shield, Wall of Fire 9 Greater Restoration, Flame Strike [!class-level|sorcerer] Level 6 [!class-feature|sorcerer] Absorb Spells Level 6 Spellfire Sorcery Feature You always have Counterspell prepared. In addition, whenever a target fails the saving throw against a Counterspell you cast, you regain 1d4 Sorcery Points. [!class-level|sorcerer] Level 14 [!class-feature|sorcerer] Honed Spellfire Level 14 Spellfire Sorcery Feature Your Spellfire Burst improves. Bolstering Flames. Add your Sorcerer level to the Temporary Hit Points granted. Radiant Fire. The damage increases from 1d4 to 1d8. [!class-level|sorcerer] Level 18 [!class-feature|sorcerer] Crown of Spellfire Level 18 Spellfire Sorcery Feature When you use Innate Sorcery, you can alter it and infuse yourself with the essence of spellfire, gaining the following benefits while that use of Innate Sorcery remains active. Once you use this feature to alter Innate Sorcery, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest unless you spend 5 Sorcery Points (no action required) to restore your use of it. Burning Life Force. Once per turn when you are hit by an attack roll, you can expend a number of Hit Point Dice up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one die). Roll the expended dice and reduce the damage taken by the total rolled. Flight. You gain a Fly Speed of 60 feet and can hover. Spell Avoidance. When you're subjected to a spell or magical effect that allows you to make a saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage on a successful save and only half damage on a failed save. You can't benefit from this feature while you have the Incapacitated condition.

title: Wild Magic Sorcery
content: [!class-header|sorcerer] Wild Magic Sorcery [!class-divider|sorcerer] [!button-pack|sorcerer] Sorcerer Sorcerer Features Sorcerer Subclasses Sorcerer Spell List Metamagic Options [!class-divider|sorcerer] !WildMagicSorcery_Banner.png [!class-recite|sorcerer] Unleash Chaotic Magic Your innate magic stems from the primal forces of chaos that underlie creation itself. Perhaps your bloodline was touched by raw magic from Limbo, the Elemental Planes, the Feywild, or some stranger source still. Whatever its origin, wild magic churns within you, waiting for opportunities to burst forth in unpredictable ways. [!class-level|sorcerer] Level 3 [!class-feature|sorcerer] Wild Magic Surge Level 3 Wild Magic Sorcery Feature Once per turn, immediately after you cast a Sorcerer spell using a spell slot, you can roll 1d20. If you roll a 20, roll on the Wild Magic Surge table to determine a magical effect. If the resulting effect casts a spell, that spell cannot be modified by Metamagic. [!class-table|sorcerer] d100 Effect 01–04 Roll on this table at the start of each of your turns for 1 minute, ignoring this result on subsequent rolls. 05–08 A friendly creature appears in a random unoccupied space within 60 feet for 1 minute. Roll 1d4: Duodrone, Flumph, Monodrone, or Unicorn. 09–12 Regain 5 Hit Points at the start of each of your turns for 1 minute. 13–16 Creatures have Disadvantage on saving throws against the next spell you cast within 1 minute that requires a save. 17–20 Roll 1d8 for a strange magical effect lasting 1 minute. 21–24 For 1 minute, your spells with a casting time of an action instead have a casting time of a Bonus Action. 25–28 You are transported to the Astral Plane until the end of your next turn. 29–32 The next damaging spell you cast within 1 minute deals maximum damage. 33–36 You have Resistance to all damage for 1 minute. 37–40 You transform into a potted plant until the start of your next turn. 41–44 You can teleport up to 20 feet as a Bonus Action on each of your turns for 1 minute. 45–48 You and up to three chosen creatures become Invisible for 1 minute. 49–52 A spectral shield grants +2 AC and immunity to Magic Missile for 1 minute. 53–56 You can take one extra action this turn. 57–60 You cast a random spell determined by a d10 roll. 61–64 Flammable nonmagical objects you touch ignite for 1 minute. 65–68 If you die within the next hour, you immediately return to life as though affected by Reincarnate. 69–72 You gain the Frightened condition until the end of your next turn. 73–76 You teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. 77–80 A random creature within 60 feet becomes Poisoned for 1d4 hours. 81–84 You emit Bright Light for 1 minute. Creatures ending their turn within 5 feet of you become Blinded until the end of their next turn. 85–88 Up to three creatures of your choice within 30 feet take 1d10 Necrotic damage. You regain Hit Points equal to the total damage dealt. 89–92 Up to three creatures of your choice within 30 feet take 4d10 Lightning damage. 93–96 You and all creatures within 30 feet gain Vulnerability to Piercing damage for 1 minute. 97–00 Roll 1d6 for a powerful beneficial effect. [!class-divider|sorcerer small] [!class-feature|sorcerer] Tides of Chaos Level 3 Wild Magic Sorcery Feature You can manipulate chaos itself to gain Advantage on one D20 Test before you roll. Once you do so, you must either cast a Sorcerer spell using a spell slot or finish a Long Rest before using this feature again. If you cast a Sorcerer spell with a spell slot before finishing a Long Rest, you automatically roll on the Wild Magic Surge table. [!class-level|sorcerer] Level 6 [!class-feature|sorcerer] Bend Luck Level 6 Wild Magic Sorcery Feature Immediately after another creature you can see rolls a D20 Test, you can take a Reaction and spend 1 Sorcery Point. Roll 1d4 and apply the result as either a bonus or penalty (your choice) to that roll. [!class-level|sorcerer] Level 14 [!class-feature|sorcerer] Controlled Chaos Level 14 Wild Magic Sorcery Feature Whenever you roll on the Wild Magic Surge table, you can roll twice and choose either result. [!class-level|sorcerer] Level 18 [!class-feature|sorcerer] Tamed Surge Level 18 Wild Magic Sorcery Feature Immediately after you cast a Sorcerer spell using a spell slot, you can choose an effect from the Wild Magic Surge table instead of rolling for one. You can choose any result except the final row (97–00). If the chosen effect requires a roll, you must still make that roll normally. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

title: Sorcerer
content: [!class-header|sorcerer] Sorcerer [!class-divider|sorcerer] [!button-pack|sorcerer] Sorcerer Summary Sorcerer Features Sorcerer Subclasses Sorcerer Spell List Metamagic Options [!class-divider|sorcerer] [!class-recite|sorcerer] A Dazzling Mage Filled with Innate Magic Sorcerers wield innate magic that is stamped into their being. Some Sorcerers can’t name the origin of their power, while others trace it to strange events in their personal or family history. The blessing of a dragon or a dryad at a baby’s birth, a brush with extraplanar forces, or even a glimpse into the hidden workings of reality might awaken a Sorcerer’s gift. Sorcerers don’t learn magic in the same way Wizards do. The raw power of magic is already part of them, and mastering that power means learning to shape, direct, and unleash it. As Sorcerers grow stronger, they become increasingly attuned to the source of their power, developing abilities that reflect the magical force flowing through their blood and soul. [!infobox-class|sorcerer wfull] Core Sorcerer Traits --- --- Primary Ability Charisma Hit Dice Die D6 per Sorcerer level Saving Throws Proficiencies Constitution and Charisma Skill Proficiencies Choose 2: Arcana, Deception, Insight, Intimidation, Persuasion, or Religion Weapon Proficiencies Simple weapons Tool Proficiencies None Armor Training None Starting Equipment Choose A or B: (A) Spear, 2 Daggers, Arcane Focus (crystal), Dungeoneer's Pack, and 28 GP; or (B) 50 GP [!class-divider|sorcerer small] [!class-header|sorcerer] Becoming a Sorcerer [!class-header|sorcerer] As a Level 1 Character Gain all the traits in the Core Sorcerer Traits table. Gain the Sorcerer's level 1 features, which are listed in the Sorcerer Features table. [!class-header|sorcerer] As a Multiclass Character Gain the Hit Point Die trait from the Core Sorcerer Traits table. Gain the Sorcerer's level 1 features, which are listed in the Sorcerer Features table. See the multiclassing rules to determine your available spell slots. [!class-divider|sorcerer small] [!class-header|sorcerer] Sorcerer Class Features As a Sorcerer, you gain the following class features when you reach the specified Sorcerer levels. These features are listed in the Sorcerer Features table. [!class-table|sorcerer] Level Prof. Bonus Class Features Sorcery Points Cantrips Prepared Spells 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 9 1 +2 Spellcasting, Innate Sorcery — 4 2 2 — — — — — — — — 2 +2 Font of Magic, Metamagic 2 4 4 3 — — — — — — — — 3 +2 Sorcerer Subclass 3 4 6 4 2 — — — — — — — 4 +2 Ability Score Improvement 4 5 7 4 3 — — — — — — — 5 +3 Sorcerous Restoration 5 5 9 4 3 2 — — — — — — 6 +3 Subclass Feature 6 5 10 4 3 3 — — — — — — 7 +3 Sorcery Incarnate 7 5 11 4 3 3 1 — — — — — 8 +3 Ability Score Improvement 8 5 12 4 3 3 2 — — — — — 9 +4 — 9 5 14 4 3 3 3 1 — — — — 10 +4 Metamagic 10 6 15 4 3 3 3 2 — — — — 11 +4 — 11 6 16 4 3 3 3 2 1 — — — 12 +4 Ability Score Improvement 12 6 16 4 3 3 3 2 1 — — — 13 +5 — 13 6 17 4 3 3 3 2 1 1 — — 14 +5 Subclass Feature 14 6 17 4 3 3 3 2 1 1 — — 15 +5 — 15 6 18 4 3 3 3 2 1 1 1 — 16 +5 Ability Score Improvement 16 6 18 4 3 3 3 2 1 1 1 — 17 +6 Metamagic 17 6 19 4 3 3 3 2 1 1 1 1 18 +6 Subclass Feature 18 6 20 4 3 3 3 3 1 1 1 1 19 +6 Epic Boon 19 6 21 4 3 3 3 3 2 1 1 1 20 +6 Arcane Apotheosis 20 6 22 4 3 3 3 3 2 2 1 1

title: Warlock Features
content: [!class-header|warlock] Warlock Feature List [!class-divider|warlock] [!button-pack|warlock] Warlock Summary Warlock Features Warlock Patrons Warlock Spell List Eldritch Invocations [!class-divider|warlock] !Warlock_Banner.png [!class-level|warlock] Level 1 [!class-feature|warlock] Eldritch Invocations Level 1 Warlock Feature You have unearthed Eldritch Invocations, pieces of forbidden knowledge that imbue you with magical abilities and occult secrets. You gain one invocation of your choice, such as Pact of the Tome. Invocations are described in the Eldritch Invocation Options section. Prerequisites. If an invocation has a prerequisite, you must meet it before selecting it. Replacing and Gaining Invocations. Whenever you gain a Warlock level, you can replace one invocation you know with another for which you qualify. You gain additional invocations as shown in the Eldritch Invocations column of the Warlock Features table. You can’t select the same invocation more than once unless its description says otherwise. [!class-divider|warlock small] [!class-feature|warlock] Pact Magic Level 1 Warlock Feature Through occult ceremony, you have formed a pact with a mysterious entity that grants you magical power. Cantrips. You know two Warlock cantrips of your choice. Whenever you gain a Warlock level, you can replace one of your cantrips from this feature with another Warlock cantrip. When you reach Warlock levels 4 and 10, you learn another Warlock cantrip. Spell Slots. The Warlock Features table shows how many Pact Magic spell slots you possess. All Pact Magic spell slots are the same level. You regain all expended Pact Magic spell slots when you finish a Short Rest or Long Rest. Prepared Spells of Level 1+. You prepare a number of Warlock spells as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Warlock Features table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional Warlock spells until your prepared list matches the number shown in the table. If another Warlock feature grants you spells that are always prepared, those spells don't count against your prepared spells. Changing Your Prepared Spells. Whenever you gain a Warlock level, you can replace one spell on your list with another eligible Warlock spell. Spellcasting Ability. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for your Warlock spells. Spellcasting Focus. You can use an Arcane Focus as a Spellcasting Focus for your Warlock spells. [!class-level|warlock] Level 2 [!class-feature|warlock] Magical Cunning Level 2 Warlock Feature You can perform an esoteric rite for 1 minute. At the end of the rite, you regain expended Pact Magic spell slots equal to up to half your maximum number of Pact Magic spell slots (round up). Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest. [!class-level|warlock] Level 3 [!class-feature|warlock] Warlock Subclass Level 3 Warlock Feature You gain a Warlock subclass (patron) of your choice. The following Warlock Patrons are available: Archfey Patron Celestial Patron Fiend Patron Great Old One Patron Hexblade Patron [!class-level|warlock] Level 4 [!class-feature|warlock] Ability Score Improvement Level 4 Warlock Feature You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Warlock levels 8, 12, and 16. [!class-level|warlock] Level 9 [!class-feature|warlock] Contact Patron Level 9 Warlock Feature You can now communicate directly with your patron. You always have Contact Other Plane prepared. You can cast it without expending a spell slot when contacting your patron, and you automatically succeed on the spell’s saving throw. Once you cast the spell this way, you can’t do so again until you finish a Long Rest. [!class-level|warlock] Level 11 [!class-feature|warlock] Mystic Arcanum Level 11 Warlock Feature Your patron grants you a magical secret known as an Arcanum. Choose one level 6 Warlock spell as your Mystic Arcanum. You can cast that spell once without expending a spell slot, and you regain that use when you finish a Long Rest. You gain additional Arcanum spells at: Level 13 (Level 7 Spell) Level 15 (Level 8 Spell) Level 17 (Level 9 Spell) Whenever you gain a Warlock level, you can replace one Arcanum spell with another Warlock spell of the same level. [!class-level|warlock] Level 19 [!class-feature|warlock] Epic Boon Level 19 Warlock Feature You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. Boon of Spell Recall is recommended. [!class-level|warlock] Level 20 [!class-feature|warlock] Eldritch Master Level 20 Warlock Feature When you use your Magical Cunning feature, you regain all your expended Pact Magic spell slots.
