title: Archfey Patron
content: [!class-header|warlock] Archfey Patron [!class-divider|warlock] [!button-pack|warlock] Warlock Warlock Features Warlock Patrons Warlock Spell List Eldritch Invocations [!class-divider|warlock] [!class-recite|warlock] Bargain with Whimsical Fey Your pact draws on the power of the Feywild. You may have forged a bargain with an Archfey such as the Prince of Frost, Titania of the Summer Court, the Queen of Air and Darkness, or an ancient hag. Others weave a web of debts, favors, and obligations among countless Fey spirits. Whatever the source of your pact, your patron is often inscrutable, capricious, and delightfully unpredictable. [!class-level|warlock] Level 3 [!class-feature|warlock] Archfey Spells Level 3 Archfey Patron Feature The magic of your patron ensures you always have certain spells prepared. When you reach the specified Warlock levels, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared. [!class-table|warlock] Warlock Level Prepared Spells 3 Calm Emotions, Faerie Fire, Misty Step, Phantasmal Force, Sleep 5 Blink, Plant Growth 7 Dominate Beast, Greater Invisibility 9 Dominate Person, Seeming [!class-divider|warlock small] [!class-feature|warlock] Steps of the Fey Level 3 Archfey Patron Feature Your patron grants you the ability to move between the boundaries of the planes. You can cast Misty Step without expending a spell slot a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. Whenever you cast Misty Step, you can choose one of the following additional effects: Refreshing Step. Immediately after you teleport, you or one creature you can see within 10 feet of yourself gains 1d10 Temporary Hit Points. Taunting Step. Creatures within 5 feet of the space you left must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or have Disadvantage on attack rolls against creatures other than you until the start of your next turn. [!class-level|warlock] Level 6 [!class-feature|warlock] Misty Escape Level 6 Archfey Patron Feature You can cast Misty Step as a Reaction when you take damage. In addition, the following options become available for your Steps of the Fey feature: Disappearing Step. You gain the Invisible condition until the start of your next turn or until immediately after you make an attack roll, deal damage, or cast a spell. Dreadful Step. Creatures within 5 feet of either the space you left or the space you appear in (your choice) must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or take 2d10 Psychic damage. [!class-level|warlock] Level 10 [!class-feature|warlock] Beguiling Defenses Level 10 Archfey Patron Feature Your patron teaches you how to shield both mind and body. You are immune to the Charmed condition. In addition, immediately after a creature you can see hits you with an attack roll, you can take a Reaction to reduce the damage you take by half (round down). You can then force the attacker to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the attacker takes Psychic damage equal to the damage you take. Once you use this Reaction, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest. You can also restore a use by expending a Pact Magic spell slot (no action required). [!class-level|warlock] Level 14 [!class-feature|warlock] Bewitching Magic Level 14 Archfey Patron Feature Your patron grants you the ability to weave teleportation into your spellcasting. Immediately after you cast an Enchantment or Illusion spell using both an action and a spell slot, you can cast Misty Step as part of the same action without expending a spell slot.

title: Celestial Patron
content: [!class-header|warlock] Celestial Patron [!class-divider|warlock] [!button-pack|warlock] Warlock Warlock Features Warlock Patrons Warlock Spell List Eldritch Invocations [!class-divider|warlock] [!class-recite|warlock] Call on the Power of the Heavens Your pact draws on the Upper Planes, realms of everlasting bliss and celestial light. You may have entered into an agreement with an empyrean, couatl, sphinx, unicorn, or another heavenly being. Others call upon a host of celestial entities whose goals align with their own. Through this pact, you gain a spark of the divine radiance that illuminates the multiverse. [!class-level|warlock] Level 3 [!class-feature|warlock] Celestial Spells Level 3 Celestial Patron Feature The magic of your patron ensures you always have certain spells prepared. When you reach the specified Warlock levels, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared. [!class-table|warlock] Warlock Level Prepared Spells 3 Aid, Cure Wounds, Guiding Bolt, Lesser Restoration, Light, Sacred Flame 5 Daylight, Revivify 7 Guardian of Faith, Wall of Fire 9 Greater Restoration, Summon Celestial [!class-divider|warlock small] [!class-feature|warlock] Healing Light Level 3 Celestial Patron Feature You gain the ability to channel celestial energy to heal wounds. You have a pool of d6s used to fuel this healing. The number of dice in the pool equals 1 plus your Warlock level. As a Bonus Action, you can heal yourself or one creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself by expending dice from the pool. The maximum number of dice you can expend at one time equals your Charisma modifier (minimum of one die). Roll the expended dice and restore Hit Points equal to the total rolled. You regain all expended dice when you finish a Long Rest. [!class-level|warlock] Level 6 [!class-feature|warlock] Radiant Soul Level 6 Celestial Patron Feature Your connection to your patron allows you to serve as a conduit for celestial energy. You gain Resistance to Radiant damage. In addition, once per turn when a spell you cast deals Radiant or Fire damage, you can add your Charisma modifier to one damage roll against one of the spell's targets. [!class-level|warlock] Level 10 [!class-feature|warlock] Celestial Resilience Level 10 Celestial Patron Feature You gain Temporary Hit Points whenever you use your Magical Cunning feature or finish a Short or Long Rest. These Temporary Hit Points equal your Warlock level plus your Charisma modifier. In addition, choose up to five creatures you can see when you gain these Temporary Hit Points. Each chosen creature gains Temporary Hit Points equal to half your Warlock level plus your Charisma modifier. [!class-level|warlock] Level 14 [!class-feature|warlock] Searing Vengeance Level 14 Celestial Patron Feature When you or an ally within 60 feet of you is about to make a Death Saving Throw, you can unleash radiant energy to preserve that creature's life. The creature regains Hit Points equal to half its Hit Point maximum and can immediately end the Prone condition on itself. Each creature of your choice within 30 feet of that creature takes Radiant damage equal to 2d8 plus your Charisma modifier and has the Blinded condition until the end of the current turn. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

title: Fiend Patron
content: [!class-header|warlock] Fiend Patron [!class-divider|warlock] [!button-pack|warlock] Warlock Warlock Features Warlock Patrons Warlock Spell List Eldritch Invocations [!class-divider|warlock] [!class-recite|warlock] Make a Deal with the Lower Planes Your pact draws on the Lower Planes, realms of damnation, corruption, and ruin. You may have forged a bargain with a demon lord such as Demogorgon or Orcus, an archdevil like Asmodeus, or another mighty fiend of terrifying power. Whatever form your patron takes, its ultimate goals are dark and destructive. Your path is defined by how closely you serve those ambitions... or how fiercely you resist them. [!class-level|warlock] Level 3 [!class-feature|warlock] Dark One's Blessing Level 3 Fiend Patron Feature When you reduce an enemy to 0 Hit Points, you gain Temporary Hit Points equal to your Charisma modifier plus your Warlock level (minimum of 1 Temporary Hit Point). You also gain this benefit if another creature reduces an enemy within 10 feet of you to 0 Hit Points. [!class-divider|warlock small] [!class-feature|warlock] Fiend Spells Level 3 Fiend Patron Feature The magic of your patron ensures you always have certain spells prepared. When you reach the specified Warlock levels, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared. [!class-table|warlock] Warlock Level Prepared Spells 3 Burning Hands, Command, Scorching Ray, Suggestion 5 Fireball, Stinking Cloud 7 Fire Shield, Wall of Fire 9 Geas, Insect Plague [!class-level|warlock] Level 6 [!class-feature|warlock] Dark One's Own Luck Level 6 Fiend Patron Feature You can call upon your fiendish patron to bend fate in your favor. When you make an ability check or a saving throw, you can add 1d10 to the roll. You can do so after seeing the roll but before any of the roll's effects occur. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once). You can use it no more than once per roll. You regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. [!class-level|warlock] Level 10 [!class-feature|warlock] Fiendish Resilience Level 10 Fiend Patron Feature Whenever you finish a Short or Long Rest, choose one damage type other than Force. You gain Resistance to that damage type until you choose a different one with this feature. [!class-level|warlock] Level 14 [!class-feature|warlock] Hurl Through Hell Level 14 Fiend Patron Feature Once per turn when you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can attempt to cast that creature through the horrors of the Lower Planes. The target must succeed on a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the target disappears and hurtles through a nightmare landscape. If the target is not a Fiend, it takes 8d10 Psychic damage. The target also gains the Incapacitated condition until the end of your next turn. It then returns to the space it previously occupied or the nearest unoccupied space. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest. You can also restore a use by expending a Pact Magic spell slot (no action required).

title: Great Old One Patron
content: [!class-header|warlock] Great Old One Patron [!class-divider|warlock] [!button-pack|warlock] Warlock Warlock Features Warlock Patrons Warlock Spell List Eldritch Invocations [!class-divider|warlock] [!class-recite|warlock] Unearth Forbidden Lore of Ineffable Beings Your pact reaches beyond mortal understanding, drawing power from entities whose motives are unknowable. You may have bound yourself to a being of the Far Realm, an elder god such as Hadar or Tharizdun, or countless alien intelligences whose whispers seep through reality. These entities may scarcely notice your existence. Yet the fragments of their forbidden knowledge grant you strange and unsettling powers. [!class-level|warlock] Level 3 [!class-feature|warlock] Awakened Mind Level 3 Great Old One Patron Feature You can form a telepathic connection between your mind and another creature's. As a Bonus Action, choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. You and the chosen creature can communicate telepathically while you remain within a number of miles of one another equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1 mile). To understand each other, both participants must mentally use a language the other knows. The connection lasts for a number of minutes equal to your Warlock level. It ends early if you use this feature to connect with a different creature. [!class-divider|warlock small] [!class-feature|warlock] Great Old One Spells Level 3 Great Old One Patron Feature The magic of your patron ensures you always have certain spells prepared. When you reach the specified Warlock levels, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared. [!class-table|warlock] Warlock Level Prepared Spells 3 Detect Thoughts, Dissonant Whispers, Phantasmal Force, Tasha's Hideous Laughter 5 Clairvoyance, Hunger of Hadar 7 Confusion, Summon Aberration 9 Modify Memory, Telekinesis [!class-divider|warlock small] [!class-feature|warlock] Psychic Spells Level 3 Great Old One Patron Feature When you cast a Warlock spell that deals damage, you can change its damage type to Psychic. In addition, when you cast a Warlock spell from the Enchantment or Illusion school, you can cast it without Verbal or Somatic components. [!class-level|warlock] Level 6 [!class-feature|warlock] Clairvoyant Combatant Level 6 Great Old One Patron Feature When you form a telepathic bond with a creature using your Awakened Mind feature, you can force that creature to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save: The creature has Disadvantage on attack rolls against you. You have Advantage on attack rolls against that creature. These effects last for the duration of the telepathic bond. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest. You can also restore a use by expending a Pact Magic spell slot (no action required). [!class-level|warlock] Level 10 [!class-feature|warlock] Eldritch Hex Level 10 Great Old One Patron Feature Your alien patron grants you a particularly potent curse. You always have Hex prepared. Whenever you cast Hex and choose an ability score, the target also has Disadvantage on saving throws made with that chosen ability for the duration of the spell. [!class-divider|warlock small] [!class-feature|warlock] Thought Shield Level 10 Great Old One Patron Feature Your thoughts cannot be read by telepathy or other magical means unless you willingly allow it. You also gain Resistance to Psychic damage. Whenever a creature deals Psychic damage to you, that creature takes Psychic damage equal to the amount you take. [!class-level|warlock] Level 14 [!class-feature|warlock] Create Thrall Level 14 Great Old One Patron Feature When you cast Summon Aberration, you can modify the spell so that it doesn't require Concentration. If you do so, the spell's duration becomes 1 minute for that casting. In addition, the summoned Aberration gains Temporary Hit Points equal to your Warlock level plus your Charisma modifier. Furthermore, the first time each turn the Aberration hits a creature affected by your Hex, it deals extra Psychic damage equal to the bonus damage granted by that spell.

title: Hexblade Patron
content: [!class-header|warlock] Hexblade Patron [!class-divider|warlock] [!button-pack|warlock] Warlock Warlock Features Warlock Patrons Warlock Spell List Eldritch Invocations [!class-divider|warlock] [!class-recite|warlock] Forge a Pact with a Cursed Blade You've made a pact with a sentient magic weapon and the cursed forces contained within its blade. Such a weapon could be the sword sheathed at your side, or it could be a manifestation of an infamous magic weapon like Blackrazor or the Sword of Kas, projecting its power to further its legend. To those willing to follow the weapon's whims, these inscrutable patrons offer the power to bestow malignant curses, deliver punishing blows, and bolster the wielder. [!class-level|warlock] Level 3 [!class-feature|warlock] Hexblade Spells Level 3 Hexblade Patron Feature The magic of your patron ensures you always have certain spells ready. When you reach the Warlock levels shown below, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared. [!class-table|warlock] Warlock Level Spells 3 Arcane Vigor, Hex, Shield, Wrathful Smite 5 Bestow Curse, Conjure Barrage 7 Freedom of Movement, Staggering Smite 9 Animate Objects, Steel Wind Strike [!class-divider|warlock small] [!class-feature|warlock] Hexblade's Curse Level 3 Hexblade Patron Feature Your patron grants you the power to curse and hinder your foes. As a Bonus Action, choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. The target is cursed for 1 minute, during which you gain the benefits below. The curse ends early if you use this feature again, dismiss it (no action required), or die. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. When you cast a spell using a spell slot that curses a target, you can use Hexblade's Curse as part of casting that spell instead of taking a Bonus Action. When you do so, the target of the spell becomes the target of your Hexblade's Curse, and the curse's duration is either 1 minute or the spell's duration, whichever is longer. Hungering Hex. When the target cursed by your Hexblade's Curse drops to 0 Hit Points, you regain Hit Points equal to 1d8 plus your Charisma modifier. Accursed Shield. While you aren't wearing armor or wielding a Shield, you gain a +2 bonus to AC while you are within 10 feet of the target cursed by your Hexblade's Curse. [!class-divider|warlock small] [!class-feature|warlock] Unyielding Will Level 3 Hexblade Patron Feature When you succeed on a saving throw to maintain Concentration, each creature of your choice in a 10-foot Emanation originating from you takes 2d6 Necrotic damage. Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until the start of your next turn. In addition, when you fail a saving throw to maintain Concentration, you can choose to succeed instead. When you do so, you gain Temporary Hit Points equal to 1d10 plus your Warlock level. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest. [!class-level|warlock] Level 6 [!class-feature|warlock] Malign Brutality Level 6 Hexblade Patron Feature Your patron's might furthers your bloodthirst, granting the following benefits: Harrowing Hex. After you cast a level 1+ spell that has a casting time of an action, you can make one attack with a weapon as a Bonus Action. Hindering Curse. When you hit the target cursed by your Hexblade's Curse with an attack roll, the target has Disadvantage on the next saving throw it makes before the start of your next turn. Inescapable Hex. When the target of your Hexblade's Curse ends its turn 30 feet or farther from you, you can move up to your Speed straight toward the target. [!class-level|warlock] Level 10 [!class-feature|warlock] Armor of Hexes Level 10 Hexblade Patron Feature When you take damage from the target cursed by your Hexblade's Curse, you can take a Reaction to reduce the damage taken by an amount equal to your Warlock level. [!class-level|warlock] Level 14 [!class-feature|warlock] Masterful Hex Level 14 Hexblade Patron Feature The power of your Hexblade's Curse increases, granting the following benefits: Accursed Critical. Any attack roll you make against the target cursed by your Hexblade's Curse scores a Critical Hit on a roll of 19 or 20 on the d20. Explosive Hex. When you deal damage to the target cursed by your Hexblade's Curse, you can cause your curse to explode with sinister energy. The target and each creature of your choice in a 30-foot Emanation originating from the target take 3d6 Necrotic, Psychic, or Radiant damage (your choice), and their Speed is reduced by 10 feet until the start of your next turn. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest unless you expend a Pact Magic slot (no action required) to restore your use of it. Hex Restoration. You regain one expended use of Hexblade's Curse when you finish a Short Rest.

title: Undead Patron
content: [!class-header|warlock] Undead Patron [!class-divider|warlock] [!button-pack|warlock] Warlock Warlock Features Warlock Patrons Warlock Spell List Eldritch Invocations [!class-divider|warlock] !UndeadPatron_Banner.png [!class-recite|warlock] Defy Death for Profane Power You've made a pact with a creature that defies the cycle of life and death: a powerful lich, a vampire, or another entity of undeath. Having once been mortal, these ancient patrons know firsthand the paths of ambition and the routes beyond death. They eagerly share this profane knowledge and other forbidden secrets with those who enact their will among the living. [!class-level|warlock] Level 3 [!class-feature|warlock] Form of Dread Level 3 Undead Patron Feature As a Bonus Action, you transform into an avatar of your patron's dreadful power. The transformation lasts for 1 minute, until you gain the Incapacitated condition, or until you end it (no action required). You can transform a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. While transformed, you gain the following benefits: Facsimile of Life. You gain Temporary Hit Points equal to 1d10 plus your Warlock level. Fearless Form. You have Immunity to the Frightened condition. If you are Frightened when you transform, that condition immediately ends. Frightful Avatar. Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can force it to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the target has the Frightened condition until the end of your next turn. [!class-divider|warlock small] [!class-feature|warlock] Undead Spells Level 3 Undead Patron Feature The magic of your patron ensures you always have certain spells ready. When you reach the Warlock levels shown below, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared. [!class-table|warlock] Warlock Level Spells 3 Bane, Blindness Deafness, Phantasmal Force, Ray of Sickness 5 Speak with Dead, Summon Undead 7 Greater Invisibility, Phantasmal Killer 9 Antilife Shell, Cloudkill [!class-level|warlock] Level 6 [!class-feature|warlock] Grave Touched Level 6 Undead Patron Feature Your patron's powers have a profound effect on your body and magic. Arcane Necrosis. Necrotic damage from your attacks, Warlock spells, and Warlock features ignores Resistance to Necrotic damage. In addition, once per turn when you cast a spell that deals damage, you can change that spell's damage type to Necrotic. Dreaded Necrosis. When you hit a creature with an attack roll and deal Necrotic damage while using your Form of Dread, you can roll one additional damage die when determining the Necrotic damage dealt. You can use this benefit only once per turn. Undead Endurance. You don't gain Exhaustion from dehydration, malnutrition, or suffocation. In addition, you don't need to sleep, and magic can't put you to sleep. [!class-level|warlock] Level 10 [!class-feature|warlock] Necrotic Husk Level 10 Undead Patron Feature Your connection to undeath saturates your body, granting the following benefits: Necrotic Resilience. You have Resistance to Necrotic damage. While using your Form of Dread, you instead have Immunity to Necrotic damage. Unholy Resuscitation. If you drop to 0 Hit Points and don't die outright, you can cause your body to erupt with deathly energy. Each creature of your choice in a 30-foot Emanation originating from you must make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, a creature takes 2d10 plus your Charisma modifier Necrotic damage. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage. Your Hit Points then change to twice your Warlock level, and you gain 1 Exhaustion level. Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a Short or Long Rest. [!class-level|warlock] Level 14 [!class-feature|warlock] Superior Dread Level 14 Undead Patron Feature Your Form of Dread improves, granting you the following benefits while it is active: Dread Resistance. You have Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage. Ghostly Flight. You gain a Fly Speed equal to your Speed and can hover. You can move through creatures and objects as if they were Difficult Terrain, but you take 1d10 Force damage if you end your turn inside a creature or an object. Profane Casting. Whenever you cast a Warlock spell from the Conjuration or Necromancy school, you cast it without Verbal, Somatic, or Material components. This benefit doesn't remove Material components that have a cost or are consumed by the spell.

title: Warlock
content: [!class-header|warlock] Warlock [!class-divider|warlock] [!button-pack|warlock] Warlock Summary Warlock Features Warlock Patrons Warlock Spell List Eldritch Invocations [!class-divider|warlock] !Warlock_Banner.png [!class-recite|warlock] An Occultist Empowered by Otherworldly Pacts Warlocks quest for knowledge hidden within the fabric of the multiverse. Through forbidden tomes, ancient rituals, and dangerous bargains, they forge pacts with powerful beings such as archfey, celestials, fiends, and entities from beyond reality. These patrons grant magical power in exchange for service, devotion, knowledge, or other mysterious obligations. While some Warlocks revere their patrons, others see them merely as tools on the path toward greater power and understanding. [!infobox-class|warlock wfull] Core Warlock Traits --- --- Primary Ability Charisma Hit Dice Die D8 per Warlock level Saving Throws Proficiencies Wisdom and Charisma Skill Proficiencies Choose 2: Arcana, Deception, History, Intimidation, Investigation, Nature, or Religion Weapon Proficiencies Simple weapons Tool Proficiencies None Armor Training Light armor Starting Equipment Choose A or B: (A) Leather Armor, Sickle, 2 Daggers, Arcane Focus (orb), Book (occult lore), Scholar’s Pack, and 15 GP; or (B) 100 GP [!class-divider|warlock small] [!class-header|warlock] Becoming a Warlock [!class-header|warlock] As a Level 1 Character Gain all the traits in the Core Warlock Traits table. Gain the Warlock’s level 1 features, which are listed in the Warlock Features table. [!class-header|warlock] As a Multiclass Character Gain the following traits from the Core Warlock Traits table: Hit Point Die and training with Light armor. Gain the Warlock’s level 1 features, which are listed in the Warlock Features table. See the multiclassing rules to determine your available spell slots. [!class-divider|warlock small] [!class-header|warlock] Warlock Class Features As a Warlock, you gain the following class features when you reach the specified Warlock levels. These features are listed in the Warlock Features table. [!class-table|warlock] Level Prof. Bonus Class Features Eldritch Invocations Cantrips Prepared Spells 1 +2 Eldritch Invocations, Pact Magic 1 2 2 2 +2 Magical Cunning 3 2 3 3 +2 Warlock Subclass 3 2 4 4 +2 Ability Score Improvement 3 3 5 5 +3 — 5 3 6 6 +3 Subclass Feature 5 3 7 7 +3 — 6 3 8 8 +3 Ability Score Improvement 6 3 9 9 +4 Contact Patron 7 3 10 10 +4 Subclass Feature 7 4 10 11 +4 Mystic Arcanum (Level 6 Spell) 7 4 11 12 +4 Ability Score Improvement 8 4 11 13 +5 Mystic Arcanum (Level 7 Spell) 8 4 12 14 +5 Subclass Feature 8 4 12 15 +5 Mystic Arcanum (Level 8 Spell) 9 4 13 16 +5 Ability Score Improvement 9 4 13 17 +6 Mystic Arcanum (Level 9 Spell) 9 4 14 18 +6 — 10 4 14 19 +6 Epic Boon 10 4 15 20 +6 Eldritch Master 10 4 15 6tc

title: Wizard Features
content: [!class-header|wizard] Wizard Feature List [!class-divider|wizard] [!button-pack|wizard] Wizard Wizard Features Wizard Subclasses Wizard Spell List [!class-divider|wizard] [!class-level|wizard] Level 1 [!class-feature|wizard] Spellcasting Level 1 Wizard Feature As a student of arcane magic, you have learned to cast spells. See the rules on spellcasting for details on how Wizard spells work. Cantrips. You know three Wizard cantrips of your choice. Light, Mage Hand, and Ray of Frost are recommended. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can replace one of your cantrips from this feature with another Wizard cantrip of your choice. When you reach Wizard levels 4 and 10, you learn another Wizard cantrip of your choice, as shown in the Cantrips column of the Wizard Features table. Spellbook. Your wizardly apprenticeship culminated in the creation of a unique spellbook. It is a Tiny object that weighs 3 pounds, contains 100 pages, and can be read only by you or someone casting Identify. The book contains the level 1+ spells you know. It starts with six level 1 Wizard spells of your choice. Detect Magic, Feather Fall, Mage Armor, Magic Missile, Sleep, and Thunderwave are recommended. Whenever you gain a Wizard level after level 1, add two Wizard spells of your choice to your spellbook. Each spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots. Spell Slots. The Wizard Features table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a Long Rest. Prepared Spells of Level 1+. You prepare the list of level 1+ spells available for you to cast. To start, choose four spells from your spellbook. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. The number of prepared spells increases as you gain Wizard levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Wizard Features table. If another Wizard feature gives you spells that are always prepared, those spells don't count against the number of spells you can prepare. Changing Your Prepared Spells. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change your prepared spells, replacing any of them with spells from your spellbook. Spellcasting Ability. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your Wizard spells. Spellcasting Focus. You can use an Arcane Focus or your spellbook as a Spellcasting Focus for your Wizard spells. Expanding and Replacing a Spellbook The spells you add as you gain levels represent ongoing magical research, but you can also discover additional spells during your adventures. Copying a Spell into the Book. When you find a level 1+ Wizard spell, you can copy it into your spellbook if it is of a level you can prepare and if you have time to copy it. Copying requires 2 hours and 50 GP per spell level. Copying the Book. You can copy spells from your spellbook into another book. This requires only 1 hour and 10 GP per spell level. If you lose your spellbook, you can recreate it by transcribing the Wizard spells you currently have prepared into a new spellbook. [!class-divider|wizard small] [!class-feature|wizard] Ritual Adept Level 1 Wizard Feature You can cast any spell as a Ritual if that spell has the Ritual tag and is in your spellbook. The spell doesn't need to be prepared, but you must read from your spellbook while casting it. [!class-divider|wizard small] [!class-feature|wizard] Arcane Recovery Level 1 Wizard Feature You can regain some of your magical energy by studying your spellbook. When you finish a Short Rest, you can recover expended spell slots with a combined level equal to no more than half your Wizard level (rounded up). None of the recovered slots can be level 6 or higher. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest. [!class-level|wizard] Level 2 [!class-feature|wizard] Scholar Level 2 Wizard Feature While studying magic, you specialized in another field of study. Choose one of the following skills in which you have proficiency: Arcana History Investigation Medicine Nature Religion You gain Expertise in the chosen skill. [!class-level|wizard] Level 3 [!class-feature|wizard] Wizard Subclass Level 3 Wizard Feature You gain a Wizard subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Wizard levels. The following subclasses are available: EN/Player Options/Classes/Wizard/Wizard Subclasses/Abjurer Diviner Evoker Illusionist Bladesinger [!class-level|wizard] Level 4 [!class-feature|wizard] Ability Score Improvement Level 4 Wizard Feature You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Wizard levels 8, 12, and 16. [!class-level|wizard] Level 5 [!class-feature|wizard] Memorize Spell Level 5 Wizard Feature Whenever you finish a Short Rest, you can study your spellbook and replace one of the level 1+ Wizard spells you currently have prepared with another level 1+ spell from your spellbook. [!class-level|wizard] Level 18 [!class-feature|wizard] Spell Mastery Level 18 Wizard Feature You have achieved such mastery over certain spells that you can cast them at will. Choose one level 1 spell and one level 2 spell in your spellbook that have a casting time of one action. You always have those spells prepared, and you can cast them at their lowest level without expending a spell slot. To cast either spell at a higher level, you must expend a spell slot as normal. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can replace either chosen spell with another eligible spell of the same level from your spellbook. [!class-level|wizard] Level 19 [!class-feature|wizard] Epic Boon Level 19 Wizard Feature You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. Boon of Spell Recall is recommended. [!class-level|wizard] Level 20 [!class-feature|wizard] Signature Spells Level 20 Wizard Feature Choose two level 3 spells in your spellbook as your signature spells. You always have these spells prepared, and you can cast each of them once at level 3 without expending a spell slot. After casting a spell this way, you can't cast it this way again until you finish a Short or Long Rest. To cast either spell at a higher level, you must expend a spell slot.

title: Abjurer
content: [!class-header|wizard] Abjurer [!class-divider|wizard] [!button-pack|wizard] Wizard Wizard Features Wizard Subclasses Wizard Spell List [!class-divider|wizard] !Abjurer_Banner.png [!class-recite|wizard] Shield Companions and Banish Foes Your study of magic focuses on spells that block, banish, and protect. Abjurers specialize in ending harmful effects, banishing evil influences, and safeguarding the weak from magical threats. They are often sought when baleful spirits require exorcism, when important locations must be warded against magical intrusion, or when portals to other planes must be sealed. Adventuring parties prize Abjurers for the protection they provide against hostile magic and devastating attacks. [!class-level|wizard] Level 3 [!class-feature|wizard] Abjuration Savant Level 3 Abjurer Feature Choose two Wizard spells from the Abjuration school, each of which must be level 2 or lower. Add those spells to your spellbook for free. In addition, whenever you gain access to a new level of spell slots in this class, you can add one Abjuration spell from the Wizard spell list to your spellbook for free. The chosen spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots. [!class-divider|wizard small] [!class-feature|wizard] Arcane Ward Level 3 Abjurer Feature You can weave protective magic around yourself. When you cast an Abjuration spell using a spell slot, you can create a magical ward that lasts until you finish a Long Rest. The ward has a maximum number of Hit Points equal to: (2 × your Wizard level) + your Intelligence modifier Whenever you take damage, the ward takes the damage instead. If you have any Resistances or Vulnerabilities, apply them before reducing the ward’s Hit Points. If damage reduces the ward to 0 Hit Points, you take any remaining damage. While the ward has 0 Hit Points, it cannot absorb damage, though its magic remains. Whenever you cast an Abjuration spell using a spell slot, the ward regains Hit Points equal to twice the spell slot's level. Alternatively, as a Bonus Action, you can expend a spell slot to restore Hit Points to the ward equal to twice the expended slot's level. Once you create the ward, you can't create another one until you finish a Long Rest. [!class-level|wizard] Level 6 [!class-feature|wizard] Projected Ward Level 6 Abjurer Feature When a creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself takes damage, you can take a Reaction to have your Arcane Ward absorb that damage. If the damage reduces the ward to 0 Hit Points, the protected creature takes any remaining damage. If the creature has any Resistances or Vulnerabilities, apply them before reducing the ward’s Hit Points. [!class-level|wizard] Level 10 [!class-feature|wizard] Spell Breaker Level 10 Abjurer Feature You always have Counterspell and Dispel Magic prepared. In addition, you gain the following benefits: You can cast Dispel Magic as a Bonus Action. You add your Proficiency Bonus to ability checks made for Dispel Magic. When you cast either Counterspell or Dispel Magic using a spell slot, the slot isn't expended if the spell fails to stop its target. [!class-level|wizard] Level 14 [!class-feature|wizard] Spell Resistance Level 14 Abjurer Feature Your mastery of protective magic grants you extraordinary defenses. You have Advantage on saving throws against spells. You have Resistance to damage dealt by spells.

title: Bladesinger
content: [!class-header|wizard] Bladesinger [!class-divider|wizard] [!button-pack|wizard] Wizard Wizard Features Wizard Subclasses Wizard Spell List [!class-divider|wizard] !Bladesinger_Banner.png [!class-recite|wizard] Wield Sword and Sorcery in Elegant Tandem Bladesingers master a tradition of wizardry that incorporates swordplay and dance. In combat, a Bladesinger uses intricate and elegant maneuvers that ward off harm while channeling magic into devastating attacks and cunning defenses. Many who witness a Bladesinger in battle remember the display as one of the most beautiful sights of their lives: a graceful dance accompanied by a singing blade. Bladesinging is strongly associated with ancient elven societies that first mastered the art and coined its name. Even today, most Bladesingers hail from old elven realms or lands shaped by elven traditions, carrying their talents across the world to aid common folk and perform heroic deeds. [!class-level|wizard] Level 3 [!class-feature|wizard] Bladesong Level 3 Bladesinger Feature As a Bonus Action, you invoke an elven magical technique known as the Bladesong, provided you aren't wearing armor or using a Shield. The Bladesong lasts for 1 minute and ends early if: You gain the Incapacitated condition. You don armor or a Shield. You use two hands to make an attack with a weapon. You can dismiss the Bladesong at any time (no action required). While the Bladesong is active, you gain the following benefits: Agility. You gain a bonus to your AC equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of +1), and your Speed increases by 10 feet. Bladework. Whenever you attack with a weapon with which you have proficiency, you can use your Intelligence modifier instead of Strength or Dexterity for attack and damage rolls. Focus. Whenever you make a Constitution saving throw to maintain Concentration, you can add your Intelligence modifier to the roll. You can invoke the Bladesong a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. [!class-divider|wizard small] [!class-feature|wizard] Training in War and Song Level 3 Bladesinger Feature You gain proficiency with all Martial Melee weapons that lack the Heavy and Two-Handed properties. You can also use any Melee weapon with which you have proficiency as a Spellcasting Focus for your Wizard spells. In addition, you gain proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: Acrobatics Athletics Performance Persuasion [!class-level|wizard] Level 6 [!class-feature|wizard] Extra Attack Level 6 Bladesinger Feature You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn. Moreover, you can replace one of those attacks with the casting of a Wizard cantrip that has a casting time of one action. [!class-level|wizard] Level 10 [!class-feature|wizard] Song of Defense Level 10 Bladesinger Feature When you take damage while your Bladesong is active, you can take a Reaction to expend a spell slot and reduce the damage by an amount equal to five times the spell slot's level. [!class-level|wizard] Level 14 [!class-feature|wizard] Song of Victory Level 14 Bladesinger Feature After you cast a spell that has a casting time of an action, you can make one weapon attack as a Bonus Action.

title: Diviner
content: [!class-header|wizard] Diviner [!class-divider|wizard] [!button-pack|wizard] Wizard Wizard Features Wizard Subclasses Wizard Spell List [!class-divider|wizard] !Diviner_Banner.png [!class-recite|wizard] Learn the Secrets of the Multiverse The counsel of a Diviner is sought by those who seek a clearer understanding of the past, present, and future. As a Diviner, you strive to part the veils of space, time, and consciousness. You dedicate yourself to mastering magic that reveals hidden truths, grants supernatural knowledge, pierces distance, and offers glimpses of what has yet to come. Through study and foresight, you become one of the greatest seekers of knowledge in the multiverse. [!class-level|wizard] Level 3 [!class-feature|wizard] Divination Savant Level 3 Diviner Feature Choose two Wizard spells from the Divination school, each of which must be level 2 or lower. Add those spells to your spellbook for free. In addition, whenever you gain access to a new level of spell slots in this class, you can add one Divination spell from the Wizard spell list to your spellbook for free. The chosen spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots. [!class-divider|wizard small] [!class-feature|wizard] Portent Level 3 Diviner Feature Glimpses of possible futures begin to intrude upon your awareness. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, roll two d20s and record the numbers rolled. You can replace any D20 Test made by you or a creature you can see with one of these foretelling rolls. You must choose to do so before the roll is made. You can replace a roll in this way only once per turn. Each foretelling roll can be used only once. When you finish a Long Rest, any unused foretelling rolls are lost. [!class-level|wizard] Level 6 [!class-feature|wizard] Expert Divination Level 6 Diviner Feature Casting Divination magic requires only a fraction of your effort. Whenever you cast a Divination spell using a level 2+ spell slot, you regain one expended spell slot. The regained slot must: Be lower in level than the slot expended. Be no higher than level 5. [!class-level|wizard] Level 10 [!class-feature|wizard] The Third Eye Level 10 Diviner Feature You can enhance your perception beyond mortal limits. As a Bonus Action, choose one of the following benefits. The chosen benefit lasts until you start a Short or Long Rest. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest. Darkvision. You gain Darkvision with a range of 120 feet. Greater Comprehension. You can read any language. See Invisibility. You can cast See Invisibility without expending a spell slot. [!class-level|wizard] Level 14 [!class-feature|wizard] Greater Portent Level 14 Diviner Feature Your visions become clearer and more accurate. Whenever you roll for your Portent feature, you roll three d20s instead of two.

title: Evoker
content: [!class-header|wizard] Evoker [!class-divider|wizard] [!button-pack|wizard] Wizard Wizard Features Wizard Subclasses Wizard Spell List [!class-divider|wizard] !Evoker_Banner.png [!class-recite|wizard] Create Explosive Elemental Effects Your studies focus on magic that unleashes devastating elemental power. Evokers specialize in spells that conjure searing flames, crackling lightning, rolling thunder, bitter cold, and burning acid. Some serve in military forces as living artillery, while others use their mastery to defend the innocent. Whatever their calling, Evokers wield some of the most destructive magic in existence. [!class-level|wizard] Level 3 [!class-feature|wizard] Evocation Savant Level 3 Evoker Feature Choose two Wizard spells from the Evocation school, each of which must be level 2 or lower. Add those spells to your spellbook for free. In addition, whenever you gain access to a new level of spell slots in this class, you can add one Evocation spell from the Wizard spell list to your spellbook for free. The chosen spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots. [!class-divider|wizard small] [!class-feature|wizard] Potent Cantrip Level 3 Evoker Feature Your damaging cantrips remain dangerous even when foes evade their full effects. When you cast a cantrip that targets a creature and: You miss with the attack roll, or The target succeeds on its saving throw, the target still takes half the cantrip's damage, if any. The target suffers none of the cantrip's additional effects. [!class-level|wizard] Level 6 [!class-feature|wizard] Sculpt Spells Level 6 Evoker Feature You can shape destructive magic around your allies. Whenever you cast an Evocation spell that affects other creatures you can see, you can choose a number of those creatures equal to: 1 + the spell's level The chosen creatures automatically succeed on their saving throws against the spell. In addition, if a successful saving throw would normally cause a creature to take half damage, those creatures instead take no damage. [!class-level|wizard] Level 10 [!class-feature|wizard] Empowered Evocation Level 10 Evoker Feature Whenever you cast a Wizard spell from the Evocation school, you can add your Intelligence modifier to one damage roll of that spell. [!class-level|wizard] Level 14 [!class-feature|wizard] Overchannel Level 14 Evoker Feature You can push your spells beyond their normal limits. When you cast a Wizard spell that: Deals damage, and Uses a spell slot of level 1 to 5, you can cause the spell to deal maximum damage instead of rolling. The first time you use this feature between Long Rests, you suffer no adverse effect. If you use this feature again before finishing a Long Rest, you immediately take 2d12 Necrotic damage per spell level after casting the spell. This damage ignores Resistance and Immunity. Each additional use before finishing a Long Rest increases the damage by an extra 1d12 per spell level. For example: Second use: 2d12 per spell level Third use: 3d12 per spell level Fourth use: 4d12 per spell level and so on.

title: Illusionist
content: [!class-header|wizard] Illusionist [!class-divider|wizard] [!button-pack|wizard] Wizard Wizard Features Wizard Subclasses Wizard Spell List [!class-divider|wizard] !Illusionist_Banner.png [!class-recite|wizard] Weave Subtle Spells of Deception You specialize in magic that dazzles the senses and tricks the mind. Through careful manipulation of perception, you create illusions that blur the line between reality and imagination. To an Illusionist, the impossible is merely something that has not yet been convincingly presented. [!class-level|wizard] Level 3 [!class-feature|wizard] Illusion Savant Level 3 Illusionist Feature Choose two Wizard spells from the Illusion school, each of which must be level 2 or lower. Add those spells to your spellbook for free. In addition, whenever you gain access to a new level of spell slots in this class, you can add one Illusion spell from the Wizard spell list to your spellbook for free. The chosen spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots. [!class-divider|wizard small] [!class-feature|wizard] Improved Illusions Level 3 Illusionist Feature Your mastery of illusion magic grants the following benefits: Silent Casting. You can cast Illusion spells without providing Verbal components. Extended Range. If an Illusion spell you cast has a range of 10 feet or greater, its range increases by 60 feet. Minor Illusion Mastery. You learn the Minor Illusion cantrip. If you already know it, you instead learn a different Wizard cantrip of your choice. This cantrip doesn't count against your number of cantrips known. When you cast Minor Illusion: You can create both a sound and an image with the same casting. You can cast it as a Bonus Action. [!class-level|wizard] Level 6 [!class-feature|wizard] Phantasmal Creatures Level 6 Illusionist Feature You always have Summon Beast and Summon Fey prepared. Whenever you cast either spell, you can change its school to Illusion, causing the summoned creature to appear spectral and illusory. You can cast the Illusion version of each spell without expending a spell slot. When cast in this way, the summoned creature has half its normal Hit Points. Once you cast either spell without a spell slot, you must finish a Long Rest before you can cast that spell in this way again. [!class-level|wizard] Level 10 [!class-feature|wizard] Illusory Self Level 10 Illusionist Feature When a creature hits you with an attack roll, you can take a Reaction to interpose an illusory duplicate of yourself. The triggering attack automatically misses you, and the illusion immediately dissipates. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest. You can also restore a use of this feature by expending a level 2+ spell slot (no action required). [!class-level|wizard] Level 14 [!class-feature|wizard] Illusory Reality Level 14 Illusionist Feature You have learned to weave shadow magic into your illusions, granting them a measure of reality. Whenever you cast an Illusion spell using a spell slot, you can choose one inanimate, nonmagical object that is part of the illusion and make it real. You can do so as a Bonus Action while the spell remains active. The object remains real for 1 minute. During that time, it: Cannot deal damage. Cannot impose conditions. For example, you could create the illusion of a bridge spanning a chasm and then make that bridge temporarily real.

content: [!class-header|wizard] Wizard Subclasses [!class-divider|wizard] [!button-pack|wizard] Wizard Summary Wizard Features Wizard Subclasses Wizard Spell List [!class-divider|wizard] [!cards|3] Abjurer Diviner Evoker Illusionist Bladesinger

title: Wizard
content: [!class-header|wizard] Wizard [!class-divider|wizard] [!button-pack|wizard] Wizard Summary Wizard Features Wizard Subclasses Wizard Spell List [!class-divider|wizard] [!class-recite|wizard] A Scholarly Magic-User of Arcane Power Wizards are defined by their exhaustive study of magic’s inner workings. They cast spells of explosive fire, arcing lightning, subtle deception, and spectacular transformations. Their magic conjures monsters from other planes of existence, glimpses the future, and forms protective barriers. Their mightiest spells change one substance into another, call meteors from the sky, or open portals to other worlds. Most Wizards share a scholarly approach to magic. They examine the theoretical underpinnings of magic, particularly the categorization of spells into schools of magic. The lure of knowledge calls even the most unadventurous Wizards from the safety of their libraries and laboratories into crumbling ruins and lost cities. [!infobox-class|wizard wfull] Core Wizard Traits --- --- Primary Ability Intelligence Hit Dice Die D6 per Wizard level Saving Throws Proficiencies Intelligence and Wisdom Skill Proficiencies Choose 2: Arcana, History, Insight, Investigation, Medicine, Nature, or Religion Weapon Proficiencies Simple weapons Tool Proficiencies None Armor Training None Starting Equipment Choose A or B: (A) 2 Daggers, Arcane Focus (Quarterstaff), Robe, Spellbook, Scholar’s Pack, and 5 GP; or (B) 55 GP [!class-divider|wizard small] [!class-header|wizard] Becoming a Wizard [!class-header|wizard] As a Level 1 Character Gain all the traits in the Core Wizard Traits table. Gain the Wizard’s level 1 features, which are listed in the Wizard Features table. [!class-header|wizard] As a Multiclass Character Gain the Hit Point Die from the Core Wizard Traits table. Gain the Wizard’s level 1 features, which are listed in the Wizard Features table. See the multiclassing rules to determine your available spell slots. [!class-divider|wizard small] [!class-header|wizard] Wizard Class Features As a Wizard, you gain the following class features when you reach the specified Wizard levels. These features are listed in the Wizard Features table. [!class-table|wizard] Level Prof. Bonus Class Features Cantrips Prepared Spells 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 9 1 +2 Spellcasting, Ritual Adept, Arcane Recovery 3 4 2 — — — — — — — — 2 +2 Scholar 3 5 3 — — — — — — — — 3 +2 Wizard Subclass 3 6 4 2 — — — — — — — 4 +2 Ability Score Improvement 4 7 4 3 — — — — — — — 5 +3 Memorize Spell 4 9 4 3 2 — — — — — — 6 +3 Subclass Feature 4 10 4 3 3 — — — — — — 7 +3 — 4 11 4 3 3 1 — — — — — 8 +3 Ability Score Improvement 4 12 4 3 3 2 — — — — — 9 +4 — 4 14 4 3 3 3 1 — — — — 10 +4 Subclass Feature 5 15 4 3 3 3 2 — — — — 11 +4 — 5 16 4 3 3 3 2 1 — — — 12 +4 Ability Score Improvement 5 16 4 3 3 3 2 1 — — — 13 +5 — 5 17 4 3 3 3 2 1 1 — — 14 +5 Subclass Feature 5 18 4 3 3 3 2 1 1 — — 15 +5 — 5 19 4 3 3 3 2 1 1 1 — 16 +5 Ability Score Improvement 5 21 4 3 3 3 2 1 1 1 — 17 +6 — 5 22 4 3 3 3 2 1 1 1 1 18 +6 Spell Mastery 5 23 4 3 3 3 3 1 1 1 1 19 +6 Epic Boon 5 24 4 3 3 3 3 2 1 1 1 20 +6 Signature Spells 5 25 4 3 3 3 3 2 2 1 1

title: Artificer Features
content: [!class-header|artificer] Artificer [!class-divider|artificer] [!button-pack|artificer] Artificer Summary Artificer Features Artificer Subclasses Artificer Spell List [!class-divider|artificer] [!class-level|artificer] Level 1 [!class-feature|artificer] Spellcasting Level 1 Artificer Feature You have learned how to channel magical energy through objects. See the Player’s Handbook for the rules on spellcasting. The information below details how you use those rules with Artificer spells, which appear in the Artificer Spell List. Tools Required. You produce your Artificer spells through tools. You can use Thieves’ Tools, Tinker’s Tools, or another kind of Artisan’s Tools with which you have proficiency as a Spellcasting Focus, and you must have one of those focuses in hand when you cast an Artificer spell (meaning the spell has an M component when you cast it). Cantrips. You know two Artificer cantrips of your choice. Acid Splash and Prestidigitation are recommended. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can replace one of your cantrips from this feature with another Artificer cantrip of your choice. When you reach Artificer levels 10 and 14, you learn another Artificer cantrip of your choice, as shown in the Cantrips column of the Artificer Features table. Spell Slots. The Artificer Features table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended slots when you finish a Long Rest. Prepared Spells of Level 1+. You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose two level 1 Artificer spells. Cure Wounds and Grease are recommended. The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Artificer levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Artificer Features table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional Artificer spells until the number of spells on your list matches the number on the table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. For example, if you’re a level 5 Artificer, your list of prepared spells can include six Artificer spells of levels 1 and 2 in any combination. If another Artificer feature gives you spells that you always have prepared, those don’t count against the number of spells you can prepare with this feature, but those spells otherwise count as Artificer spells for you. Changing Your Prepared Spells. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change your list of prepared spells, replacing any of the spells there with other Artificer spells for which you have spell slots. Spellcasting Ability. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your Artificer spells. [!class-divider|barbarian small] [!class-feature|artificer] Tinker's Magic Level 1 Artificer Feature You know the Mending cantrip. As a Magic action while holding Tinker’s Tools, you can create one item in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of yourself, choosing the item from the following list: [!class-table|artificer] Ball Bearings Flask Pouch Basket Grappling Hook Rope Bedroll Hunting Trap Sack Bell Jug Shovel Blanket Lamp Spikes, Iron Block and Tackle Manacles String Bottle, Glass Net Tinderbox Bucket Oil Torch Caltrops Paper Vial Candle Parchment Crowbar Pole The item lasts until you finish a Long Rest, at which point it vanishes. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. [!class-level|artificer] Level 2 [!class-feature|artificer] Replicate Magic Item Level 2 Artificer Feature You have learned arcane plans that you use to make magic items. Plans Known. When you gain this feature, choose four plans to learn from the Magic Item Plans (Artificer Level 2+) table. Bag of Holding, Cap of Water Breathing, Sending Stones, and Wand of the War Mage are recommended. Whenever you gain an Artificer level, you can replace one of the plans you know with a new plan for which you qualify. You learn another plan of your choice when you reach certain Artificer levels, as shown in the Plans Known column of the Artificer Features table. When you choose a plan to learn, you choose it from any Magic Item Plans table for which you qualify; your qualification is based on your Artificer level. Creating an Item. When you finish a Long Rest, you can create one or two different magic items if you have Tinker’s Tools in hand. Each item is based on one of the plans you know for this feature. If a created item requires Attunement, you can attune yourself to it the instant you create it. If you decide to attune to the item later, you must do so using the normal process for Attunement. When you reach certain Artificer levels specified in the Magic Items column of the Artificer Features table, the number of magic items you can create at the end of a Long Rest increases. Each item you create must be based on a different plan you know. You can’t have more magic items from this feature than the number shown in the Magic Items column of the Artificer Features table for your level. If you try to exceed your maximum number of magic items for this feature, the oldest item vanishes, and then the new item appears. Duration. A magic item created by this feature functions as the normal magic item, except its magic isn’t permanent; when you die, the magic item vanishes after 1d4 days. If you replace a plan you know with a new plan, any magic item created with the replaced plan immediately vanishes. If an item that you created with this feature is a container, such as a Bag of Holding, and it vanishes, its contents harmlessly appear in and around its space. Spellcasting Focus. You can use any Wand or Weapon created by this feature as a Spellcasting Focus in lieu of using a set of Artisan’s Tools. [!class-table|artificer] Magic Item Plans (Artificer Level 2+) Magic Item Plan Attunement Alchemy Jug No Bag of Holding No Cap of Water Breathing No Common magic item that isn’t a Potion, a Scroll, or cursed (you can learn this option multiple times and must select a different item each time; each item selected counts as a different plan) Varies Goggles of Night No Manifold Tool Yes Repeating Shot Yes Returning Weapon No Rope of Climbing No Sending Stones No Shield +1 No Wand of Magic Detection No Wand of Secrets No Wand of the War Mage +1 Yes Weapon +1 No Wraps of Unarmed Power +1 No [!class-table|artificer] Magic Item Plans (Artificer Level 6+) Magic Item Plan Attunement Armor +1 No Boots of Elvenkind No Boots of the Winding Path Yes Cloak of Elvenkind Yes Cloak of the Manta Ray No Dazzling Weapon Yes Eyes of Charming Yes Eyes of Minute Seeing No Gloves of Thievery No Helm of Awareness No Lantern of Revealing No Mind Sharpener Yes Necklace of Adaption Yes Pipes of Haunting No Repulsion Shield No Ring of Swimming No Ring of Water Walking No Sentinel Shield No Spell-Refueling Ring Yes Wand of Magic Missiles No Wand of Web Yes Weapon of Warning Yes [!class-table|artificer] Magic Item Plans (Artificer Level 10+) Magic Item Plan Attunement Armor of Resistance Yes Dagger of Venom No Elven Chain No Ring of Feather Falling Yes Ring of Jumping Yes Ring of Mind Shielding Yes Shield +2 No Uncommon Wondrous Item that isn’t cursed (you can learn this option multiple times and must select a different item each time; each item selected counts as a different plan) Varies Wand of the War Mage +2 Yes Weapon +2 No Wraps of Unarmed Power +2 No [!class-table|artificer] Magic Item Plans (Artificer Level 14+) Magic Item Plan Attunement Armor +2 No Arrow-Catching Shield Yes Flame Tongue Yes Rare Wondrous Item that isn’t cursed (you can learn this option multiple times and must select a different item each time; each item selected counts as a different plan) Varies Ring of Free Action Yes Ring of Protection Yes Ring of the Ram Yes [!class-level|artificer] Level 3 [!class-feature|artificer] Artificer Subclass Level 3 Artificer Feature You gain an Artificer subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Artificer levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass’s features that are of your Artificer level or lower. [!class-table|artificer] Artificer Subclasses Name Alchemist Armorer Artillerist Battle Smith Cartographer Reanimator [!class-level|artificer] Level 4 [!class-feature|artificer] Ability Score Improvement Level 4 Artificer Feature You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Artificer levels 8, 12, and 16. [!class-level|artificer] Level 6 [!class-feature|artificer] Magic Item Tinker Level 6 Artificer Feature Your Replicate Magic Item feature gains the following options. Charge Magic Item. As a Bonus Action, you can touch a magic item within 5 feet of yourself that you created with Replicate Magic Item and that uses charges. You expend a level 1+ spell slot and recharge the item. The number of charges the item regains is equal to the level of spell slot expended. Drain Magic Item. As a Bonus Action, you can touch a magic item within 5 feet of yourself that you created with Replicate Magic Item and cause the item to vanish, converting its magical energy into a spell slot. The slot is level 1 if the item is Common or level 2 if the item is Uncommon or Rare. Once you use this feature, you can’t do so again until you finish a Long Rest. Any spell slot you create with this feature vanishes when you finish a Long Rest. Transmute Magic Item. As a Magic action, you can touch one magic item within 5 feet of yourself that you created with Replicate Magic Item and transform it into a different magic item. The resulting item must be based on a magic item plan you know. Once you use this feature, you can’t do so again until you finish a Long Rest. [!class-level|artificer] Level 7 [!class-feature|artificer] Flash of Genius Level 7 Artificer Feature When you or a creature you can see within 30 feet of you fails an ability check or a saving throw, you can take a Reaction to add a bonus to the roll, potentially causing it to succeed. The bonus equals your Intelligence modifier (minimum of +1). You can take this Reaction a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. [!class-level|artificer] Level 10 [!class-feature|artificer] Magic Item Adept Level 10 Artificer Feature You can now attune to up to four magic items at once. [!class-level|artificer] Level 11 [!class-feature|artificer] Spell-Storing Item Level 11 Artificer Feature Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can touch one Simple or Martial weapon or one item that you can use as a Spellcasting Focus, and you store a spell in it, choosing a level 1, 2, or 3 Artificer spell that has a casting time of an action and doesn’t require a Material component that is consumed by the spell (you needn’t have the spell prepared). While holding the object, a creature can take a Magic action to produce the spell’s effect from it, using your spellcasting ability modifier. If the spell requires Concentration, the creature must concentrate. Once a creature has used the object to produce the spell’s effect, the object can’t be used this way again until the start of the creature’s next turn. The spell stays in the object until it’s been used a number of times equal to twice your Intelligence modifier (minimum of twice) or until you use this feature again to store a spell in an object. [!class-level|artificer] Level 14 [!class-feature|artificer] Advanced Artifice Level 14 Artificer Feature You gain the following benefits. Magic Item Savant. You can now attune to up to five magic items at once. Refreshed Genius. When you finish a Short Rest, you regain one expended use of your Flash of Genius feature. [!class-level|artificer] Level 18 [!class-feature|artificer] Magic Item Master Level 18 Artificer Feature You can now attune to up to six magic items at once. [!class-level|artificer] Level 19 [!class-feature|artificer] Epic Boon Level 19 Artificer Feature You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. Boon of Energy Resistance is recommended. [!class-level|artificer] Level 20 [!class-feature|artificer] Soul of Artifice Level 20 Artificer Feature You have developed a mystical connection to your magic items, which you can draw on for aid. You gain the following benefits. Cheat Death. If you’re reduced to 0 Hit Points but not killed outright, you can disintegrate any number of Uncommon or Rare magic items created by your Replicate Magic Item feature. If you do so, your Hit Points instead change to a number equal to 20 times the number of magic items disintegrated. Magical Guidance. When you finish a Short Rest, you regain all expended uses of your Flash of Genius if you have Attunement to at least one magic item.

title: Artificer Spell List
content: [!class-header|artificer] Artificer [!class-divider|artificer] [!button-pack|artificer] Artificer Summary Artificer Features Artificer Subclasses Artificer Spell List [!class-divider|artificer] This section presents the Artificer spell list. The spells are organized by spell level and then alphabetized, and each spell’s school of magic is listed. In the Special column: C means the spell requires Concentration. R means the spell is a Ritual. M means the spell requires a specific Material component. Artificer Spells [!class-table|artificer] Spell Name School Components Arcane Eye Divination C Fabricate Transmutation — Freedom of Movement Abjuration — Leomund's Secret Chest Conjuration M Mordenkainen's Private Sanctum Abjuration — Otiluke's Resilient Sphere Abjuration C Stone Shape Transmutation — Stoneskin Transmutation C, M Summon Construct Conjuration C, M [!class-header|artificer] title: Alchemist
content: [!class-header|artificer] Alchemist [!class-divider|artificer] [!button-pack|artificer] Artificer Summary Artificer Features Artificer Subclasses Artificer Spell List [!class-divider|artificer] [!class-recite|artificer] Craft Magic Elixirs and Potions An Alchemist is an expert at combining reagents to produce magical effects. Alchemists use their creations to give life and to leech it away. [!class-level|artificer] Level 3 [!class-feature|artificer] Tools of the Trade Level 3 Alchemist Feature You gain the following benefits. Tool Proficiency. You gain proficiency with Alchemist’s Supplies and the Herbalism Kit. If you already have one of these proficiencies, you gain proficiency with one other type of Artisan’s Tools of your choice (or with two other types if you have both). Potion Crafting. When you brew a potion using the crafting rules in the Dungeon Master’s Guide, the amount of time required to craft it is halved. [!class-divider|artificer small] [!class-feature|artificer] Alchemist Spells Level 3 Alchemist Feature When you reach an Artificer level specified in the Alchemist Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared. [!class-table|artificer] Artificer Level Spells 3 Healing Word, Ray of Sickness 5 Flaming Sphere, Melf's Acid Arrow 9 Gaseous Form, Mass Healing Word 13 Death Ward, Vitriolic Sphere 17 Cloudkill, Raise Dead [!class-divider|artificer small] [!class-feature|artificer] Experimental Elixir Level 3 Alchemist Feature Whenever you finish a Long Rest while holding Alchemist’s Supplies, you can use that tool to magically produce two elixirs. For each elixir, roll on the Experimental Elixir table for the elixir’s effect, which is triggered when someone drinks the elixir. The elixir appears in a vial, and the vial vanishes when the elixir is drunk or poured out. If any elixir remains when you finish a Long Rest, the elixir and its vial vanish. Drinking an Elixir. As a Bonus Action, a creature can drink the elixir or administer it to another creature within 5 feet of itself. Creating Additional Elixirs. As a Magic action while holding Alchemist’s Supplies, you can expend one spell slot to create another elixir. When you do so, you choose its effect from the Experimental Elixir table rather than rolling. When you reach certain Artificer levels, you can make an additional elixir at the end of each Long Rest: a total of three at level 5, four at level 9, and five at level 15. [!class-table|artificer] D6 Effect 1 Healing. The drinker regains a number of Hit Points equal to 2d8 plus your Intelligence modifier. The number of Hit Points restored increases by 1d8 when you reach Artificer levels 9 (3d8) and 15 (4d8). 2 Swiftness. The drinker’s Speed increases by 10 feet for 1 hour. This bonus increases when you reach Artificer levels 9 (15 feet) and 15 (20 feet). 3 Resilience. The drinker gains a +1 bonus to AC for 10 minutes. The duration increases when you reach Artificer levels 9 (1 hour) and 15 (8 hours). 4 Boldness. The drinker can roll 1d4 and add the number rolled to every attack roll and saving throw it makes for the next minute. The duration increases when you reach Artificer levels 9 (10 minutes) and 15 (1 hour). 5 Flight. The drinker gains a Fly Speed of 10 feet for 10 minutes. The Fly Speed increases when you reach Artificer levels 9 (20 feet) and 15 (30 feet). 6 You determine the elixir’s effect by choosing one of the other rows in this table. [!class-level|artificer] Level 5 [!class-feature|artificer] Alchemical Savant Level 5 Alchemist Feature Whenever you cast a spell using your Alchemist’s Supplies as the Spellcasting Focus, you gain a bonus to one roll of the spell. That roll must restore Hit Points or be a damage roll that deals Acid, Fire, or Poison damage. The bonus equals your Intelligence modifier (minimum bonus of +1). [!class-level|artificer] Level 9 [!class-feature|artificer] Restorative Reagents Level 9 Alchemist Feature You can cast Lesser Restoration without expending a spell slot and without preparing the spell, provided you use Alchemist’s Supplies as the Spellcasting Focus. You can do so a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. [!class-level|artificer] Level 15 [!class-feature|artificer] Chemical Mastery Level 15 Alchemist Feature You gain the following benefits. Alchemical Eruption. When you cast an Artificer spell that deals Acid, Fire, or Poison damage to a target, you can also deal 2d8 Force damage to that target. You can use this benefit only once on each of your turns. Chemical Resistance. You gain Resistance to Acid damage and Poison damage. You also gain Immunity to the Poisoned condition. Conjured Cauldron. You can cast Tasha’s Bubbling Cauldron without expending a spell slot, without preparing the spell, and without Material components, provided you use Alchemist’s Supplies as the Spellcasting Focus. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

title: Armorer
content: [!class-header|artificer] Armorer [!class-divider|artificer] [!button-pack|artificer] Artificer Summary Artificer Features Artificer Subclasses Artificer Spell List [!class-divider|artificer] [!class-recite|artificer] Craft Magic Armor to Enhance Your Abilities An Armorer modifies armor to function almost like a second skin. The armor is enhanced to hone the Armorer’s magic, unleash potent attacks, and generate a formidable defense. [!class-level|artificer] Level 3 [!class-feature|artificer] Tools of the Trade Level 3 Armorer Feature You gain the following benefits. Armor Training. You gain training with Heavy armor. Tool Proficiency. You gain proficiency with Smith’s Tools. If you already have this tool proficiency, you gain proficiency with one other type of Artisan’s Tools of your choice. Armor Crafting. When you craft nonmagical or magic armor, the amount of time required to craft it is halved. [!class-divider|artificer small] [!class-feature|artificer] Armorer Spells Level 3 Armorer Feature When you reach an Artificer level specified in the Armorer Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared. [!class-table|artificer] Artificer Level Spells 3 Magic Missile, Thunderwave 5 Mirror Image, Shatter 9 Hypnotic Pattern, Lightning Bolt 13 Fire Shield, Greater Invisibility 17 Passwall, Wall of Force [!class-divider|artificer small] [!class-feature|artificer] Arcane Armor Level 3 Armorer Feature As a Magic action while you have Smith’s Tools in hand, you can turn a suit of armor you are wearing into Arcane Armor. The armor continues to be Arcane Armor until you don another suit of armor or you die. You gain the following benefits while wearing your Arcane Armor. No Strength Requirement. If the armor normally has a Strength requirement, the Arcane Armor lacks this requirement for you. Quick Don and Doff. You can don or doff the armor as a Utilize action. The armor can’t be removed against your will. Spellcasting Focus. You can use the Arcane Armor as a Spellcasting Focus for your Artificer spells. [!class-divider|artificer small] [!class-feature|artificer] Armor Model Level 3 Armorer Feature You can customize your Arcane Armor. When you do so, choose one of the following armor models: Dreadnaught, Guardian, or Infiltrator. The model you choose gives you special benefits while you wear it. Each model includes a special weapon. When you attack with that weapon, you can add your Intelligence modifier, instead of your Strength or Dexterity modifier, to the attack and damage rolls. You can change the armor’s model whenever you finish a Short or Long Rest if you have Smith’s Tools in hand. [!class-table|artificer] Armor Model Features Dreadnaught Force Demolisher, Giant Stature Guardian Thunder Pulse, Defensive Field Infiltrator Lightning Launcher, Powered Steps, Dampening Field [!class-divider|artificer small] [!class-feature|artificer] Dreadnaught Armor Model You design your armor to become a towering juggernaut in battle. It has the following features. Force Demolisher. An arcane wrecking ball or sledgehammer projects from your armor. The demolisher counts as a Simple Melee weapon with the Reach property, and it deals 1d10 Force damage on a hit. If you hit a creature that is at least one size smaller than you with the demolisher, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from yourself or pull the creature up to 10 feet toward yourself. Giant Stature. As a Bonus Action, you transform and enlarge your armor for 1 minute. For the duration, your reach increases by 5 feet, and if you are smaller than Large, you become Large, along with anything you are wearing. If there isn’t enough room for you to increase your size, your size doesn’t change. You can use this Bonus Action a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. [!class-divider|artificer small] [!class-feature|artificer] Guardian Armor Model You design your armor to be in the front line of conflict. It has the following features. Thunder Pulse. You can discharge concussive blasts with strikes from your armor. The pulse counts as a Simple Melee weapon and deals 1d8 Thunder damage on a hit. A creature hit by the pulse has Disadvantage on attack rolls against targets other than you until the start of your next turn. Defensive Field. While Bloodied, you can take a Bonus Action to gain Temporary Hit Points equal to your Artificer level. You lose these Temporary Hit Points if you doff the armor. [!class-divider|artificer small] [!class-feature|artificer] Infiltrator Armor Model You customize your armor for subtler undertakings. It has the following features. Lightning Launcher. A gemlike node appears on your armor, from which you can shoot bolts of lightning. The launcher counts as a Simple Ranged weapon with a normal range of 90 feet and a long range of 300 feet, and it deals 1d6 Lightning damage on a hit. Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with the launcher, you can deal an extra 1d6 Lightning damage to that target. Powered Steps. Your Speed increases by 5 feet. Dampening Field. You have Advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks. If the armor imposes Disadvantage on such checks, the Advantage and Disadvantage cancel each other, as normal. [!class-level|artificer] Level 5 [!class-feature|artificer] Extra Attack Level 5 Armorer Feature You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn. [!class-level|artificer] Level 9 [!class-feature|artificer] Improved Armorer Level 9 Armorer Feature You gain the following benefits. Armor Replication. You learn an additional plan for your Replicate Magic Item feature, and it must be in the Armor category. If you replace that plan, you must replace it with another Armor plan. In addition, you can create an additional item with that feature, and the item must also be in the Armor category. Improved Arsenal. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with the special weapon of your Arcane Armor model. [!class-level|artificer] Level 15 [!class-feature|artificer] Perfected Armor Level 15 Armorer Feature Your Arcane Armor gains additional benefits based on its model, as detailed below. Dreadnaught The damage die of your Force Demolisher increases to 2d6 Force damage. When you use your Giant Stature, your reach increases by 10 feet, your size can increase to Large or Huge (your choice), and you have Advantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws for the duration. Guardian The damage die of your Thunder Pulse increases to 1d10 Thunder damage. When a Huge or smaller creature you can see ends its turn within 30 feet of you, you can take a Reaction to magically force that creature to make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, you pull the creature up to 25 feet directly toward you to an unoccupied space. If you pull the target to a space within 5 feet of yourself, you can make a melee weapon attack against it as part of this Reaction. You can take this Reaction a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. Infiltrator The damage die of your Lightning Launcher increases to 2d6 Lightning damage. Any creature that takes Lightning damage from your Lightning Launcher glimmers with magical light until the start of your next turn. The glimmering creature sheds Dim Light in a 5-foot radius, and it has Disadvantage on attack rolls against you, as the light jolts it if it attacks you. As a Bonus Action, you can gain a Fly Speed equal to twice your Speed until the end of the current turn. You can take this Bonus Action a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

title: Artificer Subclasses
content: [!class-header|artificer] Artificer [!class-divider|artificer] [!button-pack|artificer] Artificer Summary Artificer Features Artificer Subclasses Artificer Spell List [!class-divider|artificer] [!cards|3] Alchemist Armorer Artillerist Battle Smith Cartographer Reanimator

title: Artillerist
